college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_title: The Life at the most prestigious Instititute (IIT BOMBAY)
review_text: Pros/Cons: The students team here in IIT BOMBAY is very good.  The seniors are ready to help in anything you want. The campus is also peaceful and filled with greenary. You wouuld definitely enjoy the place here. The infrastucture is pretty good but not that good. The lecture halls are far from the hostel so it will take atleast 30 mins to walk. The professors even though are good at the subject they lack the ability to explain it clearly. The classes are really huge, which makes the class always distractive. | Placement Experience: The placements in IIT bombay are one of the best in the whole country due to it's high packages, But recently many students are doing MTech and MBA from foreign rather than going for direct jobs . Some are also going for starting businesses.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text: Pros/Cons: professor are knowlegeable and supportive with strong academic and research background. campus is vibrant with diverse clubs, fests and engaging peer interaction. infrastructure is excellent with beautiful hostels, labs and classrooms. The girls to boys ratio is low and especially in core branches. core placements are limited for some interdisciplinary departments. The academic workload is high especailly during exams. | Placement Experience: Placement begins with 7th semester in our college.Top companies like microsoft, google and TATA etc visit our campus for campus placement. highest goes into crores and the average is around 21 to 25 LPA in on campus placement. In my case I choose to persuie higher education rather than going for placement just after my Btech completion

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text: Pros/Cons: So many events, no only fests but competions are also held weekly for many things. Each competetion teches you something new. The curriculum and research opportunties are outstanding. Tech teams, gives you hands on experience and also you if you have any innovative idea yoyu can try it out. Girls to boys ratio is 1 to 4. Could have been better Some games like free fire or pubg does not run on the wifi. You need a VPN to run such games. Some time mess food is not tasty but you still have canteen. | Placement Experience: Campus placement is offered. In Fact most student opt for campus placement only. You can apply for off campus placement as well. The reputation of college is such that it helps in placement a lot. Students are eligible for campus placement after the 7th semester. I am planning to do MS in aerospace from a reputed foriegn university.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text: Pros/Cons: This is the top most IIT which provides placement approx 23 lpa. Asia's biggest cultural event is organised by our college. This is in Mumbai so various fields are open. Far from home and whether is very humid and Harsh. Since Mumbai is a very expensive place so it is bad for students. Hostel closing time is 10:00 p.m. which I personally don't like. | Placement Experience: Our college placement is till now the best in India where computer science branch is having a average package of 30 lacs and core branches get 19lpa. Companies like Google Microsoft Accenture Oracle visit our campus. Almost average student gets place here

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_title: Life at IIT Bombay : In and Out
review_text: Pros/Cons: The boys to girls ratio is now 4:1 which which is a significant improvement from the past The infrastructure is world class and constant improvements keep taking place continuously. There are various student and faculty mentorship programs, to ensure that the mental and physical health of the students is taken care of. The hostel culture in girls hostels is pretty less. The involment and engagement and bonding of boys hostel has improved. The course curriculum is too rigourous, and needs a lot of effort for outstanding results. The salaries data that comes out is not 100 percent true, 1/2 people get these super high packages and rest are average and a bit above. | Placement Experience: Semester 7 is when the placement season starts, and sem 5 onwards you can appear for internship season. The highest package goes in crores but mostly in cse department, others not IT jobs have reletively lower packages avarage being 20 LPA. Almost 60 percent and above students get placed in my guess. I am planning to work in a chemical process design firm.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The amount of resources iit bombay provides is awesome. The professors clarity over the subject they teach and their intuitive explanation skills are good. The clubs and tech teams provide deep domain knowledge and keep us constantly active in some or the other events. The hostel services can be improved and also the mess food . There can be more interactive sessions so that the students are able to know each other. The transport facilities in the college can be improved. | Placement Experience: The campus placements at iit Bombay are good , we can apply for any company irrespective of our branch
Students are eligible for placements from the 7th semester onwards 
There are no companies that visited iit bombay but did not offer
The highest package ever recorded was around 4.5cr, it was in Fintech ( developing algorithms)
75-80 percent of the students get placed every year
My plan is to sit for placements in the companies iam interested in after my degree

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_title: Everything is best in iit bombay
review_text: Pros/Cons: Mess food:- different types of food provided, even we have snacks in evening Hostel room:- rooms are larger then average room, contains table and chair also Medical facilities:- you can use medical facilities free of cost. Transport:- sometimes we not able to get perfect Transport for travelling to nearest station Internet problem:- sometimes full internet got shut down Cleaning problem:- sometimes workers not able to keep hygiene properly | Course Curriculum Overview: Ya this cousre can have good scope ha future because aerospace have so much scope in industry. Courses are well aligned to our best development towards this field. Frequency and timings are well enough difficultly level is intermediate. Improvement can be that more real life problems can be solved.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text: Pros/Cons: Got a really good infrastructure and facilities for academic activities The sporting facilities available are top notch Most of the professors are the best in their field and consultants for reputed organizations | Placement Experience: The college has a placement cell which tried to being the best companies and offers to our students each year. We get internship opportunities in our 3rd year and main placement in 4th year(mainly at the end of 7th sem). Most of the big tech companies(microsoft, google, amazon), consulting companies(McKinsey, BCG) to a name a few comes to our college each year. Around 75% students were placed last year(some choosing higher studies instead). The highest package last year was 3.61 crores and the average was around 22 lakh

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_title: For the one who are looking for affordable and quality this is the best
review_text: Pros/Cons: It is very affordable and not much formalities or any limitations and all as you know its a technical based collage The person who running the collage is very kind and trying to help everyone he can I personally very crazy about his behave i never see he making any issues regarding the late fees with any one this is the most important I have nothing much to say about negatives, still the size of the class rooms and all should be little more big Also the staffs they are not much supportive or trying to help the principal he struggles sometimes They can still get some more facilities like mess or food stations near, | Placement Experience: They have already informed us regards the placements and details of the places if we reach the criteria also i have seened 4-5 percentages of the students get the placements as i told you he was running the collae with kindness and help

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Mumbai
review_text: Pros/Cons: classes are very big. the professors are excellent and everything you have learnt will be tested atleast once in 2-3 weeks. quality of the infrastructure is excellent and no other indian colleges have been this much beautiful. asia's largest techfest and cultural fest happen here which describes the brand of of the college. as per some reports we are hearing that placements are being reduced. girls to boys ratio is 1:4 which is a disappointment according to me. | Placement Experience: as far of my knowledge the plancements are the best than compared to any other college in india but the placents are decreasing as of the reports we got.average package is around 15-20 lakhs and highest is around 4 crore per annum.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: Great placement record with good package String alumini network across industries Top notch infrastructures, lab and hostel High academic pressure and competitive environment Hostel and mess maintenance is required at times. Flood normally occurs during rainy season | Placement Experience: From 7th sem start we qualify for campus placement. Nearly 800 offers made with nearly all big MNCs and many good startup and all best Indian companies. Highest package-1.80 Cr by Tower for quantative analyst role and avg package of 12Lakhs

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus is completely different from the whole city, it is more cleaner and is quiet peaceful with various different beautiful views at different points and is not too big so could easily walk through the campus. Has really great architecture of the buildings and is surrounded by really intelligent people including some over the top profs and some really great minds from the country. The night life is completely different and is one of the best part of staying in campus. some of the hostels are quiet old and need renovation. | Placement Experience: If you study passionately and have decent communication skills and some important competencies, I assure you that you will secure a position in a great company. Here at IIT Delhi, you earn what you work for; you can receive salary packages as high as 2 crores, but some offers start as low as 4 lakhs per annum.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Peer group, faculty and people in general are very good and worth learning from Maintenance of campus and division of labor is very good Pro-student atmosphere is there with adequate help and support The courses are sequenced with bottleneck courses which if failed can disturb everything Mess food quality is good in absolute sense but hygiene issues still noticeable Certain faculty members are unruly and have to be dealt with care | Placement Experience: Students appear for placement exams in the beginning of their fourth B.Tech year. Many companies like MAANG, Optiver, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, Accenture, BCG visit the campus with number of offers ranging from single digit (say 2) to double digits (say 30).
Highest package I am aware among my friends: 90 LPA, average: 15 LPA. 90% students get placements.
I plan to do a corporate job after completing my degree.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: The college infrastructure and facilities. Laboratories for nearly all fields of physics The course load can be heavy some times. Curriculum followed is too old and need to be revised | Placement Experience: Btech sudent become eligible for placements after their sixth semester. Placement tests start in the seventh semester and interviews are conducted in winter break. Lot of companies come to our campus such as Google, Microsoft, Graviton, Da vinci, Adobe, Oracle, Goldman Sachs, etc.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_title: Another Point of View for IIT Delhi students life .
review_text: Pros/Cons: I liked their course curriculum and management. They are student friendly , some professors are very friendly. Many events are student driven only , that is important for management skills. The one and most disliked thing is that they don't even give 1 day gap in exams . Sometimes their attendance policy is very hectic. | Course Curriculum Overview: Hmm , the course structure designed by them is very accurate and they first teach that course and then make compulsory to attend lab of same course in coming semester, this ensures that whatever we have learnt , we can apply that in real life as well.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: The classes and the labs are very well managed and are very advanced. The time table prepared by the proffs is  really good and we get  a lot time to study and do extra curricular activities The social interections with juniors as well as seniors is very nice in our college. Due to it we get to know more people and improve our speaking skills The faculties are very good in their respective subject with a great knowledge, also we have good placement in our college and we are also guided for it The only thing i disliked is our hostels are very old as IIT Delhi is a very old college so new hostels must be made in our college | Placement Experience: In the 4th year that is after 7th sem you become eilgible for campus placements. Actually people get jobs before they get their college degree!!  Various big companies visit our college for college placement like google, microsoft, nestle, meta ,infosis etc. As i talk about my branch 90% student get placement in my college with an average package of 1500000 and highest package in my branch is 2 Crore. My plan after degree is to simply do a nice job.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: First of all the fest in the college are really very awesome..There is a cultural fest that occur once a year (Rendezvous). This is a must watch fest for all the new upcoming students. The sports culture at iit delhi is much more engaging and developed... People compete in the tournament names as GC(general championship)... Seniors help us to learn and play new sports which we never played earlier... Hostel and it's life is really very awesome and loved to spend... The food in the mess is well managed by a committee of each hostel.. and seprate apartments have given to the mess staff so that there is no problem in working of the mess. The one thing I dislike is the quality of teaching of the professors.. The lectures are sometimes really very boring to attend and students eventually lose interest in attending lectures and are much dependent on self study. The second thing I dislike is about the size of the campus.. yes it is about 340 acres. But in comparison to other iits it is small. And hence there are not as big playground or activity centres or halls as compared to other iit s. The third thing I dislike is about the fact that except of cycle no other vehicle is allowed for the students living in hostel...There are many big fines imposed if someone is caught ...Around 10000 if get caught with bike... | Course Curriculum Overview: The course at IIT Delhi are well prepared and well organised for the students... Especially for the first year students .. after first year one can choose and drop a course.. as he/she wish... There are some some course that are more theoretical that should more about pratical knowledge...the exams at iit delhi are very frequent I mean there might be a quiz or a viva or some kind of demo each coming week .. so We have to be prepared for each and every thing that is upcoming.... The difficulty of exam are moderate to difficult... But it largely depend on the professor of the course (course coordinator)....

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus infrastructure is quite good even if campus size is the smallest considering all iits but the infrastructure is quite unique and appreciable The cultural activies and sports are quite well established. The seniors really know what they are doing and help the juniors to follow the path. The girl to boy ratio is quite good due to girl reservation. It is about 1:3 The quality of hostels are quite bad . Not recommendable The relevance of subject taught in first year is obsolete Transition from teachers to proffessors is very difficult . Teaching methods are very bad. Student teacher interaction is none in the class | Course Curriculum Overview: Most of the people who choose iit delhi non circuital branches dont worry about the curriculum only thing that matters is the college. Curriculum is nowhere related to real word. It has no real world application. The courses have gone obselete the institute need to redesign the course . There are total 4 exams in one course 2 quic minor and major. The difficuly varies depending on the professor

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_title: Life of a non-local Student at IIT delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: The environment- nice plantations and comparatively less pollution than the city. The classerooms- very big and futuristic classes with ACs and projectors The events- many events like TRYST for techfest, RDV for cultural fest. The language barriers- most of the people speak Hindi, so the assumed language of conversation is Hindi between TAs and students or sometimes Teachers too. Fast paced classes for slow learners compared to the toppers Larger classrooms suggests less interaction between students themselves as well as with teachers. | Placement Experience: This is one of the benifits of joining an IIT. Placements and internships are conducted by Office career services-OCS at IIT Delhi. The salaries are kinda low but the employability is good. Women get the jobs a little bit easier compared to Men due to the companies rule. I got placed but the salary is low. I am thinking to leave the job in 2 years and prepare for Civils exam to become an IAS officer.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD], New Delhi
review_text: Pros/Cons: Good collage and good maintaining all good. Faculty are perfect good teaching in the faculty Good placement and good package in the collage No holidays sum times full boring in class room Summer holidays is only 12 day so iam so sad Sum subjects are understand but sum subjects are not understand | Placement Experience: Then the our college good placement and good package starting package 6 lakhs high package 58 lakhs studying is good automatically good package is there good placements are there in our college and good studying

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_title: Life at IIT Madras by Sagnik Chakrabarty
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus was very eco-friendly with deer and monkeys roaming around. The professors of my department are very helpful and friendly. They help us as much as they can. There are 3-4 reading halls in our campus. Some are open 24X7. They are beneficial for students who like to study during night. The mess food of our college is not so good. | Placement Experience: From the end of their 3rd year, students become eligible for placements. Many companies visited our campus and the total number of offers were around 400. Some notable companies are Google, Microsoft, Adobe and Amazon. Anout 60% of the students get placed. After getting the degree, I may go to some company or may do some research in Chemical Engineering.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text: Pros/Cons: I think our college has the best technical infrastructure in the country. Our tech fest is bigger than most not just in India but in Asia. Our robotics department has developed AI enabled robots. Hostels are not that good as we still have common washrooms. | Placement Experience: Apollo Hospital to Greens and many medical and hospital which are engaged in hospitals or building technology that supports it. Our course is still not that old and in the batches that have passed out so far, I think the average stands at some 10 lakhs or so.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_title: IITM BS DEGREE - a game changer in distant education
review_text: Pros/Cons: I liked about the study team, the professors the instructors. They are all very knowledgeable. I also liked about the structure of the syllabus. The all courses are of 12 weeks. Also, all the students are connected by the G chat. We are having the houses different houses from the different regions. None I do not dislike anything about the BS DEGREE | Placement Experience: After the sixth test students become eligible for the campus placements, there are many companies. Many high-level companies who visit the campus like the Microsoft, Google, Adobe, and TCS Infosys and many more the number of marriage, I can tell you the ratio, one students get approximately 8-9 offers. The highest package was approximately 1.0 2CR.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: the teachers at iit madras are very knowledgeable and supportive especially in helping students with research , projects and career choices the green campus , great infrastructure. the campus life with plenty of clubs , fests and sporting events. the gender balance  is highly uneven , with far fewer girls than boys certain administrative procedure , including approvals and hsotel formalities. the academic load at times is extrermely demanding | Placement Experience: placements at IIT madras are really good , form  9th semester onwards, comapnies like microsoft and google come for placement , with the heighest offer beimg about 4.3 cr and 
 average 20-30lakhs. about of 90% students get placed .  and i want to work in biotechnology  or research .

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: No strict rules are followed , people can give exams even they get P grades but they cannot hold any position of responsibility Semester is only for 4 months , will have 2 months semester holiday with hostel accomodation if required No curfew for students , entering and leaving campus is ease. At main part of chennai so feasible to phoneix mall, Velachery , Guindy , yaramani 4 months long semester holiday , profs are getting restricted amount of classes so they aren't able to teach at best AC is not available in 90% of classrooms . 2-3 events which u really need to attend happens at same time | Placement Experience: People at end of  second year start to apply for internship,
It is college with Highest package ever received in India 400lpa.
People do intership at summer break after 6th sem and sit for placement exams in 7,8th sema.

Placement team of IIT madras is vibrant community.pulls jobs with good pacakges

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The IITM campus is clean and green, it's basically built inside a forest, so the atmosphere is very peaceful. The infrastructure is really good and the lab facilities are wonderful. All faculties are PhD scholars and are well versed in their domain I do not like the teaching methodology of some of my faculties, even though they are highly knowledgeable. There will be too many events and activities happening at the same time, so it will be a bit difficult to manage academics and social life Sometimes, the exams will be on back to back days with less time to study | Placement Experience: The placement opportunities are plenty at IITM. Since I am in my first year, I don't know much about placements but I have heard that top companies around the world come for placements.
The highest package this year was 4.3 crore per annum. The average salary in Electrical engineering is 22 LPA.
I plan to join a company and work for a few years and then decide what to do. I am not yet clear about my future plans.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Professors are well qualified and helpful. We get very nice alumni network after completing this degree We get all india connection in this college because from each state students are there Gender ratio in this course is very bad need to be improved. Connections to a the professor is also not good Although, this is a online degree so we don't get much interaction with fellow classmates | Placement Experience: Student can get intership after second year for placement students are eligible after 3rd year.
Average package is almost 9 to 10 lakh whis is decent for new course. Still better than private colleges because roi is also good

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text: Pros/Cons: Our instructors are very good they teach in depth Daily we have online classes by instructor and teachers assistant I love offline fest , so much fun i wait each year for that Less interaction with professor which makes difficult for us to connect with them | Placement Experience: Placement are good i guess i don't know much about placement cell but i see news about placement regularly so im sure student are get placed, after my degree I'll go for foreign for study in master in data science

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Though it's a online course but you get chance to go offline for fest They provide internship and placements with good package They provide live classes every day which are very useful where you can clear doubt Recorded Lecture are boring and so I take live lecture only by instructors Sometimes it hard to concentrate because everything is online | Placement Experience: This course is new but many students are getting good placement, our average placement is around 10lakh , our placement cell is working good each year we see increase in placements. Mostly students crack off campus placement and also some secure it from on campus 
If I get good marks in gate I'll do my master or I'll work as a data scientist.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Chennai
review_title: A honest Review about my college, Academics, opportunities, campus life
review_text: Pros/Cons: The things that I liked about my college is we have good social interconnection with everyone. We have many opportunities available inside our campus which helps  us to have extra curricular activities. Also my college has a vast area so there are all the facilities available like any labs which are needed for every batch and have a football, basketball, badminton court and ground as well. Students can have relaxation time by involving in many activities and also the way is teaching is very nice which helps many of our seniors to conquer a good job having a good salary package. The things that I dislikes about my college is very few like there are only minimum number of classes available. We have class once or twice in a week. The other dislike the contacts with other department students is very less since we need travel a long distance to reach other departments. | Placement Experience: The placements in our college is very good and having many companies which are offering high salary packages. Google,amazon, flipkart and many other higher companies have come to our college for campus placement. The students of our college are passionate and more than forty to fifty percentage of students get campus placement.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: The freedom that the college provide is so immence. We can litterally go out anytime we want without any disturbance from the authority The time given for working on self is also a positive thing. We are litterally given 4 months out 12 as a holidays or breaks to work on your skills. The placements of the college is so good. The Academic is very tough. You have to grind those 4 months real hard in order to get good grades | Placement Experience: So the placements of IITK is so good. As it is one of the top colleges of engineering in india, the placement here are quite good. The Average pakages according to last year stats is somewhere around 22 lakhs. And then the we have branch-wise average Pakages too. The students becomes elgible for interhsip in his/her 5th semester and for the placement in his/her 7th semester. The American Express & Jp Morgon visited IITK and has made 11 and 6 offers respectively. The percentage of students getting placed are high , usually higher than 85 percent. I planned on preapring for UPSC after the my degree.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Very good academic environment and highly qualified faculty Well-maintained labs, hostels, library, and sports facilities are available. Effective career development cell with great placements. The 1:6 girls-to-boys ratio affects social balance on campus The quality of food could be significantly improved. Some courses are too theory-heavy and could benefit from more practical content. | Placement Experience: Placements at IIT Kanpur are excellent, especially in core engineering, software, analytics, and finance sectors . Students become eligible for placements from 7th semester(B.Tech).
Top recuiters include Google, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, McKinsey, and Texas Instruments , startups and PSUs like IOCL and ISRO also visit.
Highest package was around 1.5Cr/annum, while avg package for mechanical is 18Lpa.
Over 84% of eligible students get placed .
After my degree , I plan to pursue higher studies .

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Our syllabus is in line with top international universities as we have most syllabus inspired by them. Our infrastructure is better than every single IITs of the country and is also one of the largest IITs of the country. Our course is as tough as it gets as because Chemical engineering is one of the most complex studies but we are given teaching that makes this complex studies looks simple. Definitely it's some of the students as they enter IITs and then lose all their seriousness. | Placement Experience: At IIT Kanpur the standards are same and the pattern is set as well. So, from the last year we have less studies and all placements activities at top. From Alchem Labs to Ipca Labs many listed companies at NSE are the ones that offers placements and percentage for Chemical engineering is 86% as of now.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Faculty have superb record in research and development Infrastructure is world class and constantly under development Overall society in diverse and excellent peer group. Everywhere post graduate students have not adequate representation Academics is super tough and some irrelevant cousrses are there There is some sort of drug abuse happening in some hostels | Placement Experience: The placement drive start in 7th sem with interviews at the end of 7th semester and another in 8th sem. Around 80% of students gets placed mostly in non core sector like SDE, finance, consulting. Average package is around 25lpa, with maximum around 4.3cr.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus scenery consisting of wide range flora and fauna sparks the beauty of IIT Perfect library with all the books needed for best education Hostels are decent with a diverse range of sport facilities Mess food is not so good due to a lot of accommodation | Course Curriculum Overview: The course curriculum is almost perfect with proper exams conducted throughout the semester to test the knowledge of students and rectify their mistakes. But few courses are outdated in 1st year which creates a negative impact on academics.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Girls to boys ratio in our department is 1:3 The faculty here is top notch and world renowned professors. The course curriculum is very efficient and useful for future The coursework is very heavy with lots of quiz, exams, assignments which can sometimes feel like a lot. The routine is very hectic with lots of classes throughout the day The mess food is not good, sometimes very oily and bad taste. | Course Curriculum Overview: The course is very theory heavy with less practical implications. Their should be some courses in sem 1 or 2 to help students prepare for placements. Also course pressure can be reduced so that students can practice for placements.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons:   | Placement Experience: From 7 th semester students become eligible for campus placement. 
Many companies visited like nvidia, oracle, optiver, graviton, amazon ,flipkart,Meesho, zomato etc
Highest package I know is approximately 1.5 cr internationally.
Almost 90 percent are placed.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Iit kanpur also known for high academic standards.The faculty is well qualified. Iit kanpur provides good scientific and technological research facility.There are many projects working on Ai, Robotics etc in Iit kanpur. It provides many intership program. It also gives many scholarship program.It collaborates with many top rank universities of the world. There is so much pressure of academic like viva,exam etc.It can lead to stress many times.The workload in B tech is so high on students. The campus is well mentioned but nearby it so much pollution. There is many factory working near it. The climate in kanpur is harsh.during summer temperatures Crossed 40 degree Celsius. It leads to so much stress. | Placement Experience: It has strong and successful placement record.there are many sectors where good placement going on in iit kanpur like it, consulting,core engineering, manufacturing, startups etc.top most companies like Amazon Facebook,apple flipkart actively recruiting from iit kanpur.A Number of student got pre placement during internships.average package at Iit kanpur is 24 lakh .top package at Iit kanpur is approximately 1 cr per annum.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: The big campus is the thing i liked the most Many eateries inside the campus which serves food uptill 4 in the night The oat that hosts so many different functions Sometimes the bathroom services are not good as there is a problem of water Mess food gets boring after some time due to repetitio Academics is sometimes too much hectic here | Placement Experience: Placements here at IIT KANPUR are improving year by year and try to place as many students as possible . With a large team of students and professors that work day and night to invite companies for campus recruitment.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK], Kanpur
review_title: IIT-K ka tempo high hai. Come join this premier institute for a great college life and beyond.
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is huge, and green all around. You'll enjoy summer evenings sitting at the CCD. Most of the professors are great at what they do. You get to make friends with the best folks. IITK is a bit too strict on academics than some of the other IITs. Kanpur being a smaller corporate centre than Delhi or Mumbai; students get a bit less exposure for internships. Like in any other college, some professors are not as great as others. | Placement Experience: Placements sessions are organised by students run placement cell. They work round the clock for the benefit of the students community. You will get workshops and tutorials that will get you ready for placements.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: INDIAN INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY KHARAGPUR : A EXPERIANCE OF FIRST YEAR STUDENT.
review_text:
Pros/Cons: ACADEMIC ENVIRONMENT AND FACULTY : Academics in IITKGP is highly well described and well enriched atmosphere and mostly all professors are supportive in all fields . Campus Life And Opportunities : Campus of IITKGP is 2100 Acre in area Which makes it largest IIT College and campus life is full of opportunities in academic and extra activities like sports, Tech, Cultral, events and many more.There is a lot of fun. INFRASTRUCTURE AND LEARNING FACILITIES : The infra is excellent, with full equipped labs with modern machines and tools, advanced reaseach facilities, library with huge amount of books of every subject and field. Hostel : There are 21 hostels in college out of which most of which are older than 30 years which need maintenance and upgradation most frequent problem of water shortage is shit for students. Mess  : Every mess have different mess but food and meal in all hostel not satisfy students daily need as mostly there is paneer and potato only and variety is very narrow and whenever we complain for that there is always a excuese of budget. Distance And Transport within campus : A huge campus is also a disadvantage for students which leads in waste of time during going to class and time table is such that we have to travel upto 6-7 km per day(as hostel to class room again and again). | Placement Experience: Placement process in IIT KGP is well designed and functioned every year placements are done.Generally from seventh semester placement process is started for student and every student is eligible in campus placements as per requirements of company and according to their demand students are selected for final process of interview.
Multiple company visit the campus annually and some companies visits campus are Microsoft,Google,Apple,Amazon,Infosys,Tata,Mahindra,Reliance and many hundreds of company more.
No. of offers made depends on requirement of company.
percentage of students selected for placement vary every year but appr. 70% studenst get placemets and rest apply for off campus placements as per their interest.
avg. package of IIT KGP is 19,00,000.
highest package is 3,65,00,000.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: First class infrastructure and everything well designed Academics management are good with cirricular and extra curricular activities Professors are very helpful to each and every student Girls boys ratio is unbalanced 1 is to 4 as per the seats Placements support is good but they could make it better to reach every student | Placement Experience: Placmenets in iit kgp is quite good and high compared to other iits or other engineering colleges many companies visit our college and mostly reputed compaines like amazon microsoft and honeywell comes and many students got selected through the college placement. 
80% of the students are getting placed through off campus and on campus

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: THE KGP, THE TEMPO, THE PEACE
review_text: Pros/Cons: IIT Kharagpur doesn't discriminate among girls and boys. all the rules and regulations are same for both the gender. Campus has a very beautiful night life you can roam inside the campus with your friends on a cycle and explore campus beauty IIT Kharagpur has a very good infrastructure it has 2100 acre campus and provides facilities like a central library which operates 8am to 12pm. poor connectivity form the city like if you want to catch a flight you have to go to kolkata that is around 110 km from campus. Being the oldest iit some buildings are very old and need maintenance hostel rooms feels very old and boring. some times people feels very loaded academics and i feel that pressure is quit higher here | Placement Experience: For placement and internship we have CCD ( Career development center ) and it ensure that students are getting all the opportunities for interview with top companies like Microsoft, google, Goldman Sache amazon and so on. students can apply for internship in 5th semester and can sit for placement in their 8th semester, other than campus placement students can apply anywhere for off campus placement if they are meeting thier crieteria.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: A BALANCED JOURNEY: ACADEMICS,CAMPUS LIFE & GROWTH
review_text:
Pros/Cons: faculty support and infrastructure wise and placement assistance improvement in  hostel and canteen facilities everyone are engaged each other we have different clubs to interact each other with seniors also placement services are very good they support us in each and every aspect administrative delays some process like subject registration approvals it took longer than expected | Placement Experience: palcements of my college is quite good every yesr more students are getting placed  on campus 1)students who are enrolled in btech they are eligible in the 4th year in 7 th sem .
no of offers are depending on the year because last year 1800 offers we get and compaines like microsoft ,flipkart,amazon,honeywell, samsung etc....

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: M.Tech in IIT KGP - What you need to know.
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is vibrant with lush greenery. Students are culturally active with multiple opportunities to showcase their talent. Multiple opportunities for growth with frequent workshops and seminars. Research enthusiasts will find an array of different top of the line equipments with mostly good guidance. Hostel infrastructure is not well maintained. Triple sharing rooms are also being provided to m.tech students. Mess food is bad and not always hygienic Some faculties have let their job get to their heads and have quite the ego problem. | Placement Experience: The placement scenario in IIT KGP is great if you're a B.Tech student. For M.Tech the placements are good but only in other departments like ME, CSE and CHE. Mostly students pursue research after M.Tech.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Very good academic infrastructure for students and teachers alike. Students are given a lot of freedom regarding their non academic life. Many other interests/hobbies can be pursued due to the facilities available. The curriculum is old and dated, so may not be of as much use in current world scenario,. The mess food varies from hostel to hostel, so your experience may very from your peers. Extremely huge campus leading to long cycle journeys everyday to class. | Placement Experience: The placement situation at IIT KGP is one of th e best among Engineering Colleges in India. With an average package of around 2200000 and overall placement percentage of around 80 (B.Tech+M.Tech).
Students become eligible for placement either if they get PPO(Pre Placement Offer) from the company they are interning in in the 5th semester or when they are in the 7th semester when placement season begins.
Around 1800 offers and 400 PPOs were made in phase 1 which includes 25 international offers. There were around 400 companies that visited. The highest offer was 21400000.
Top profiles are Software, Data, Finance, Consulting, Quant and Core.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Culture of KGP is good.the campus is so beautiful the best thing Biggest IIT campus and the first IIT always a flex My friends here. The life here will be horrible if you dont have good few friends The professors.some professors has knowledge about the subject but has no idea to teach The hostel food is horrible some days.depends on your hall.wish that you get a good hall at 2nd year There is a thing called illumination in which the hall which has tempo .the students of that hall should do lot of work. Seniors will drag us into it.we cant escape from that | Placement Experience: Placements depends on your skills
Usually there is a compulsory internship at the end of 3rd year
If you didnt get it via college you should do it from outside but its compulsory
And at the end of 4th year you get placement.
Placemeng isnt 100% . If you have the skill you will get placed thats the criteria.many people say you are in IIT your life is sorted .but that aint the reality

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: IIT KGP in a sophmores eye
review_text: Pros/Cons: The departmental profesors are good and friendly The sports facilities are really nice and anyone can go and participate The people here are also very friendly and chill The placement pressure is very high and not everyone is getting good placements The ac facility is not available in hostels The food was not good in the freshers hostel | Placement Experience: The students can apply for internship via Career Development Centre in the beginning of their pre final Year. they can go for off campus intern whenever they want. Most of the goo companies would come and mostly for software finance consulting etc.  The placements start in the second last semester as far as i know. The competition for placement is getting really high these days

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Liked the Classroom Environment so much , there is centralised Ac in all the classes along with sufficient lights which keeps the focus while studying . The Central Library which is considered one of the largest technical library of Asia is also fully AC with thousands of books and has the perfect studying environment. Moreover, the Campus area which the largest holding 2100 acres is full of greenery and peace which not only calm the mind but also the body. | Placement Experience: This course offers 4 semesters which a 2 year course and anyone who is pursuing this course is eligible for the placements after successfully completing his first year course work. On an average approx 40-50% of the student get placed and those who dont get off campus placements.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP], Kharagpur
review_title: IIT KHARAGPUR KA TEMPO HIGH HAI
review_text: Pros/Cons: Vibrant Campus life, clean and teady campus and worlds best minds as our respected faculty members. The college life is full of joy and enthusiasm Lot of events occurring all over the year. Every weekend there is something great happening inside campus to ingage in. Not dislike but yaa you will have to study a lot Sometimes it becomes tough to find good food inside campus. It's quite costly inside the campus everything is overpriced. | Placement Experience: The  placement records at IIT Kharagpur is quite impressive. All the final year students gets chance to appear in the placements. Many renowned companies like Google Microsoft qualcom Tata motors Maruti Suzuki etcetra are some companies that come in IIT Kharagpur for placement. college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text: Pros/Cons: I loved the culture between seniors and junior interaction. Also the professors Professors and their work area and other interdisciplinary exposure Research opportunities in core available at even bachelors level Placements and companies in core is going to be limited For this branch the core companies pay scale is less The labs could be improved at international level in comparison | Placement Experience: In the third semester itself the students are eligible for internships and at the beginning of 4 year they apply this it for placements the placement committee circulates emails to the student for the different companies and their criteria and then the students can choose the companies write their test and sit for internship and placements

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_title: The feeling of being in IIT Roorkee
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus here is the best thing. It’s amazing no matter how old it is. Its Green all over and fully maintained. The Great Thomson Building which is the pride of our IIT Roorkee looks heavenly all the time and mostly in rain There is a separate course assigned to students called Tinkering and Mentoring which is allowing students to learn and innovate no matter how much mistakes you make you will succeed and learn The management here in every event is bad. There are not enough people doing there job during events | Placement Experience: Placements are good as you all know its IIT. The avg package of all branches is still around 20 LPA . For CS its around 35-40 LPA. And for all other branches its greater than 15 LPA. Im a first year student so i have not seated in placement but there are so many companies that visit iit roorkee for placements, like google,microsoft, ibm, oracle, etc
Highest package secured by a student this year is 1.5 crore.
Nearly 95 percent of students get placed every year for sure.
My plans after degree is to get placed and do my job.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text: Pros/Cons: The beauty of the campus is commendable. The sports facility existing in the campus is great. You wish to play a sport, you will get the facilities here. Enormous number of clubs in the campus give great exposure. Temperature variation is insane. It rains one minute and the next minute you have eye-piercing sunlight. | Placement Experience: There is a dedicated placement drive that is handled by Placement & Internship Cell organized in final year during the first semester. There are provisions for students wishing to persue further studies.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is very beautiful. Its iit roorkee so peers are super smart and innovative. There are multiple societies so you can follow your passion. Quality of teachers is very good . They are very intelligent and have a lot experience in teaching field. Sports facilities are very good. There is gym and swimming pool too It's location in Roorkee town of haridwar so it's far from metropolitan areas Those who don't like rainy days , may feel a little bad here in monsoon. Hustle culture sometimes makes you feel bad . | Placement Experience: Iit roorkee provide better Placement opportunities than any other college. It has it's placement and intership cell . Gold man Sachs, JP Morgan Chase, graviton and quantbox are the big recruiters which visit my college. Placement are good with average package of 1800000 per annum. If someone don't get on campus then cell helps in getting off campus placement. Enormous amd strong alumini network helps in getting off campus placement.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text: Pros/Cons: Student at IIT Roorkee brings diverse culture. Great scope of networking among students. Night life at IIT Roorkee is very nice there. Unhealthy competition among students are there. Higher load of study among the students. Few prof have different teaching techniques. | Placement Experience: Placement of IIT roorkee is actually very good, in first year most of the students had multiple offer for internship and final placement is also very nice as around 25 percent of student gets Pre placement offer.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text:
Pros/Cons: the sports facilities are nice enough and you can explore stuff the campus is beautiful your eyes will be soothed the professors mostly are nice and approachable first sem ke bekar the gender ratio is 4 boys to 1 girl in campus which ain't much there is no boxing training place here in campus first year hostel rooms are not very good and not equal treatment for all, random but still | Placement Experience: placements are decent as obviously this is an IIT that too top 5 old IIT, roorkee it is
average may be around 20 circuital branches getting the average to the higher side which is kinda obvious keeping in mind market

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text: Pros/Cons: There are world class facilities in our college one of them is a tinkering lab where we have motion capture room that coated about 2crores. Our campus life is great with a constant stream of events happening and a lot of exposure . We have a very great placement and internship cell that helps student in placements and interns. I think that there should be free buses inside the campus as it is big so i donot have to pay for e-rickshaws. One that i would also like is installation of air conditioners in hostels. There should be an option to choose if you like to avail mess or not as the food isn't good and one can eat cheap food in canteens as there are subsidized a lot. | Placement Experience: Our college has a very good placement ratio and placement also depends on the economy and industry that year and a lot lf other factor but if you have a CGPA of above 8 you are moat likely to get placed. The highest package ranges from 2-3 crores and average goes around 13lpa. I am currently planning to do an MBA after graduation from a foreign university moat likely Harvard.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The professors are highly educated and well versed with fields and have exciting opportunities for the students The placement and internship and opportunities are very good with a very good pay The campus culture in all types of technical fields like Web development, Competitive Programming, Product Management, AI/ML is very good The hostels are a bit old so students might struggle to adjust The mess food is average and it is quite hard to survive on mess food alone The campus administration has a somewhat backward mindset like forcing compulsory attendance and not having enough attention on mental health of the students | Placement Experience: From 7th semester the students are eligible for placements.
Many Companies like Google, Microsoft, Quantbox, Adobe, JP Morgan come to our college and on an average hire 6-8 students per company.
Average packages were around 15 LPA.
More than 80% of the students who applied got placed.
I will pursue MBA after my degree

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_title: I enjoyed to give the review with you
review_text:
Pros/Cons: There is freedom to go anywhere and no academic pressure. We are having a good placement percentage and package. There are a lot of cultural activities will held here in every month. The ratio of boys and girls here is 1:4. | Placement Experience: Normally for 7 th semester most of the students here will qualify in placements with good packages. There are many international companies like Google , amazon , Schlumberger etc are here to give placements for students.
Me after getting the degree definitely I will go to good company which pays me good salary.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR], Roorkee
review_title: Enjoy your best part of life
review_text: Pros/Cons: Yes this collage is provided all the needs of a student for their improvement. There is full facilities in hostel and mess. For mentorship there is wellness centre. There is a carrier development cell for the development of students The most disliking thing the hectic nature of this collage which is become the cause of stress | Placement Experience: I am in my first year so I don't know much about it but as per I listen from my senior is that around 70 to 80 percent students got the placement in civil engineering branch I think thats a very good data as per my expectations

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: Hostels are also clean and good. There are 13 hostels , which includes 10 boys , and 3 girls hostels. Academic is quite tough here, profs are good in teaching . college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text:

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_title: A clear review of iit Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is very peaceful and beautiful . campus is big.it got 3 lakes in it . I feel this is the most beautiful iit.very green The sports facilities are damn good. college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: Well i liked the management amd staff, their behaviour But sometimes they take 2-3 days to reply | Course Curriculum Overview: The course curriculum is quite good, like all subjects are properly arranged, in a perfect manner they should be teached like in a proper order, if something we need to know in trimester 3 that will be teached in trimester 1 or 2

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: The exposure to every thing including dance music art Placement and internship opportunities are good enough Highly qualified professors from different. IIT and foreign universities Competitive environment , lot of competition among students Girls to boys ratio is 1:4 and this is quite less than other colleges Location is 16 km away from the city because of the big campus | Placement Experience: The placement is quite good in my college. In 7 th semester the placements start and the package goes from 16lpa to 2 cr . About 75 percent student who applied got placed . I want to work in a software firm.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus life is very good, u get a lot of exposure here, there are many clubs and activities where you can learn new things and explore. The college girls to boys ratio is around 1:4.5.Most of the faculty are very good atleast have completed Phd from highly reputed universities in the country However students engagement with faculty is not as much as in other colleges, College has nice infrastructure and advanced technologies, curriculum is also good allows you to focus to learn other skills as well Hostel food isnt upto the mark, most of the cuisine tastes like north-east which is little difficult for other students Placement in core branches are not so great but good in other circuital branches The climate in guwahati is little unpredictable , rains frequently , Summers are very hot and humid while winters are cold with fog | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for placements from 7th semester, few students also get pre placement offer from their interships after their 6th semester or in the mid 7th semester. Almost 70% of placement rate in our department with hifhest package around 60lpa and median is 17lpa

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_title: IIT GUWAHATI from a first yearites eyes
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is really peaceful serene and beautiful. The hostel culture is really really strong and that leads to full Chichorre vibes during the GC and is indeed my favourite time of the semester Something or the other is always happening, whether it be a competition or some fest . There is never a dull moment The academics are really really heavy and demanding The attendance policy of 75% is also followed to a T. The weather during the monsoon semester is horrid. The humidity will want to you to enter your room and never come out | Placement Experience: As an IIT the placements are ofcourse top of the line.
Although my branch is a lot more inclined towards academia but still students can apply for jobs of any field
The opportunities to learn different skill is truly endless

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Overall environment is pretty natural and green Events and fun sessions throughout the semester Interaction with Alumnus, Professor, Friends Campus is far from the city but institute provides Bus facilities Less training and mentoring from Placement Cell Bad sports facilities within hostels and less library books in the hostels | Placement Experience: From the seventh semester, one is eligible for Placements. This year(2024-25) 68 percent placement is done for B.Tech. 
Those who don't get placements either go for higher studies, UPSC, GATE, and some get job by referral from College Alumni.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Very good campus environment: Bring a closed campus you get to interact with like minded people. Depite being closed campus and far from guwahati city you get access to all major services at subsidized rates. There are various transport options too. Student clubs: we have a range of students build and managed clubs in various fields of tech, cultural, sports and welfare Infrastructure: you get good and well maintained hostel rooms, sports facilities and student activity centres, libraries and auditorium. Academic: there is a strict attendance policy that inhibit you from taking more extracurricular activities and built something of value Placement: It is not as good as the likes of IITB or IITD. Some good companies do not visit us especially in non tech roles Funds for activities: Getting funds for building something in student clubs is very tiresome and difficult. | Placement Experience: Placement were pretty good. If you have a good profile then you wont struggle to find a job. Though the number of recruiters and avg package is lower than that of other IITs, but still you can expect a 20 to 30lk job on Day 1 of Placement season with is at par with other IITs. Though the number if such recruiters is not massive but is good enough for almost everyone. 

Placement stats:
Avg percent of students getting placed: 80 to 100%
Avg package:
Day 1: 30 to 40lk
Day 2: 15 to 25lk
Day 3: 10lk
Post Day 3: 5 to 10lk
Overall: 10 to 12lk

Note: most btech students get placed before day 3

Number of recruiters: 200+
Number of offers made: 4000+

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Guwahati
review_text: Pros/Cons: Quality of infrastructure is awesome. They renew frequently Campus is beautiful also have a name as  the most beautiful campus of all iits. review_text: Pros/Cons: The infrastructure is very good with japnese collaboration buildings Friendly faculties and subject matter experts. review_text: Pros/Cons: The thing that I like the most is the infrastructure. All the buildings are really well built. There is no hostel curfew. college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI], Indore
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Infrastructure is as best as it gets. Single occupancy rooms for hostels, excellent central dining facility, spacious classrooms, etc. Faculties are very talented and experienced, a lot of things to learn from them under research. Placement services are great, provides ample opportunities for students. Infrastructure is good but not quality wise, the quality of construction is poor. Having difficulties in monsoon Course curriculum is not that great. It should be according to industry needs, just like other IITs While some faculties are great, some are just existing there without much doing. Their sole purpose seems to just exist there without contributing much. | Placement Experience: Placements in my college are very good in one sense but okayish in some other. Many companies visit the college but only some of them are good and if one loses opportunity in some top companies, then only low paying companies remain.
Students become eligible for college placements after 6 semester.
Some top companies visiting for placements include, Goldman Sachs, Databricks, Google, Zomato, Amazon, etc. All of the students in my branch have received their offers except one or two, who will receive it eventually.
Highest package this year is 1.04Cr INR and average would fall around 30L INR.
Percentage of students getting placed in my branch is around always 100%.
My plans after a degree is to work in industry offered to me by my placements, but even if I don't get on campus placement, I would be seeking out off campus placements and I am positive, I'll be in good place.

college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI], Indore
review_text: Pros/Cons: The fact that IIT Indore was a comparatively newer IIT at the time made it possible for students to access newly built hostels and amenities not usually easily found at any older IIT. The campus was quite beautiful and offered a lot of amenities. Although IIT Indore was 5 years old at the time of my admission, reputed companies visited the campus for placements and it didn't feel like an uphill task to achieve good placement figures. The curricular as well as the extra curricular environment at campus was quite wonderful. All facilities related to sports, library, gym, jogging, etc. were provided which helped everyone relax and learn at the same time. The mess food quality (included along with hostel accommodation) was not up to the mark. There were several vendors who were changed in a matter of few months, and this process was undertaken every year. | Placement Experience: The placement season at IIT Indore for my batch was quite spectacular with over 90% placement achieved across all three branches. The placement season that begins in November pans out till March depending on the company schedule. Some of the companies that visited the campus include DE Shaw, Amazon, John Deere, ISRO, BPCL, Texas Instruments, Flipkart, etc. While the average package was around INR 8 lacs per annum, the highest went up till INR 45 lacs per annum. While I had secured a placement with one of the leading companies in the industry, I chose to pursue MBA as I had an admit from IIMA.

review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus infrastructure is very good and full of greenery. Faculties are very helpful, experienced and highly educated. Hostels are very good with air conditioning. college_name: IIT Gandhinagar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG], Gandhinagar
review_title: From Campus Life to Academics: My insights into IIT Gandhinagar
review_text: Pros/Cons: IIT Gandhinagar has a high-tech campus. AC are fitted in each and every room. Lights are automated everywhere. Every floor of the hostel a kitchen room for students which have a induction cooker, microwave oven and a fridge. Instructors are very active here. All teachers try their best because college takes review of the teachers from the students at the end of the semester. It provides course of History, film making, biology to give it's students a taste of every field I personally likes the sports facilities of the college the most. It has a big football and cricket ground. Many indoors courts for game like volley, basketball, badminton, etc. It has a indoor swimming pool and a fully air conditioned gym. There is network problem in this area. If you just want network just for internet this is not a problem because wi-fi is there but during calling sometimes sound lags. First year students has to attend PE classes with a min attendance of 80% and 2nd year students have to attend PE with min attendance of 50%. The celebration here are not so tempting as of college like IITB. I find ganeshpuja, navratri, etc very dull. | Placement Experience: In 2024, IIT Gandhinagar has seen a recession in the placement. About 164 students have registered and only 125 got a job. The average package was Rs. 18.20 LPA. The average package of B.Tech CSE students was around 22.73 LPA. Students are elligible for campus placements in their 7th semester in which companies like infosys, amul, adani group, NVIDIA comes. Top companies like microsoft and google have not came yet. This year on of the 4th year B.Tech electrical student got a job offer of 48 LPA which is amazing.

review_text: Pros/Cons: Good peer group, good and supportive professors, good infrastructure, super good coding culture, good senior-junior interaction. fests are really good here. college_name: IIT Patna - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITP], Patna
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Indian Institute of Technology-Patna has a very beautiful campus. For first years the hostel is too spacious. The relation between juniors and seniors is too good. The grading system. This college has absolute grading system. The rooms in hostels for 2nd,3rd years are too small. The classes starts early nearly at 8 am . | Placement Experience: In phase 1 alone 207 students were placed,including 58 pre placement offers. Impressively, 12 international offers were made. The average package of Indian Institute of Technology stood at ?25.5LPA,where the highest domestic package reached 60LPA.Top recruiters this year are GOOGLE 15 offers ,FLIPKART, ZS ASSOCIATES,MICROSOFT ,AMAZON, ADOBE, INTEL , NVIDIA ,and ACCENTURE JAPAN.
Departmentwise, M.Sc Mathematics program achieved 100%placement, artificial intelligence and data science 80% and computer science and engineering 75%. Aproximately 700 students participated for placements this year and participation of over 220 companies.

review_text: Pros/Cons: I like the Transportation system there are 4 buses that run inside the campus  time to time. Curriculum is well designed all the lectures run freely no hectic schedules. Labs are outdated I think we are wasting time for simple and basic applications, we do want to learn complex things taught in the lectures. Library is small it is always packed and our department library is being used as a storage room for new equipment. | Placement Experience: From 5th semester students are eligible for placements, around 50 companies came to IIT Patna for placement drive and 60+ companies visited for hiring interns. As of I know by January 2025 nearly 80% of 4th years were placed and still it is going on these numbers might change there are aroung 500 plus fourth year students and 600 plus 3rd year students. Highest package was over 60LPA and average is 25LPA for over all placement drive till January(per company is not disclosed to 3rd years yet so I am not sure for individual companies) I am things about higher studies may be I might pursue M.Tech in Electrical Engineering

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Mandi
review_title: Nurturing Minds in the Himalayan Foothills: A review of IIT Mandi
review_text: Pros/Cons: Stunning Campus and Natural Beauty: IIT Mandi is renowned for picturesque campus nestled amidst the Himalayas. Strong Academic Focus and Rigorous Curriculum: The institute offers a challenging academic curriculum designed to equip students with the necessary skills and knowledge to excel in their chosen fields. Supportive Faculty and Mentorship: IIT MAndi boasts a dedicated faculty comprisiing experience researches and industry experts who are committed to guiding student academic and personal growth. The institute's location ins a remote are can sometime pose challenges in terms of accessibility and connectivity , especially for students who prefer a more urban environment. while the campus is beautiful, it man lack certain amenities and facilities that are common in larger urban campuses. The hilly terrain can lead to extreme weather conditions, which may affect outdoor activities and campus life during certain seasons | Placement Experience: Company from Microsoft , Google, Adobe amazon, Atlassion, BNY Mellon,uber. InMobi, Samsung and aroung 100 more company visit college every year for Internship as well as placements. For Btech CSE average package is aroung 22LPA .

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Mandi
review_title: IIT Mandi- my home and karmbhoomi
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is situated in the Himalayas which has its own merits. Natural Beauty with unpolluted air. The weather here is quite nice, you will find yourself in state of aw after seeing through window after rain, clouds stuck amidst the big mountains. If you are from circuital branch then you have better chances of getting good placement as mostly all companies allow all the circuital branches. The academic section is slow and work like normal government office. Most rules for academics are not in favor of students. They require amendments especially in cases of approval after getting internship. Hostel facilities are worst where the college don't have enough of infrastructure and admitting large no. of students as herds. In a room where only 1 people can fit have been provided to 2 students. Similarly 4 students are forced in room of 3. Transport facilities are worse where if you have to attend class in north campus and you have to go from south then there are 80 % chances that bus will fill and you will miss the class. On special permission few busses have been added. | Placement Experience: Students are eligible for placement from 7th semester.

Some of the big tech giants visit us like google , Microsoft, Oracle, Samsung, Texas Instruments, Uber, Paytm, walmart, Flipkart, amazon, etc. Offers made in 2022-23 are more than 150 where company count was more than 70. 
Highest package is around 63 LPA (INDIA) whereas more than 1 CR (Out of India). Average package is around 33 LPA in 2022-23. Almost 80%+ students sitting in placement gets placed (according to 2022-23 data). My plan is to join tech firm either as SDE or Data Scientist.

review_text: Pros/Cons: Sports grounds are of decent size and with excellent weather it's fun to play in them Classrooms are very nicely built, though now the space is less, so the newer ones are being made is a hurry Their are a lot spots near here to visit in 2-3 days break. college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Mandi
review_title: The Honest Review Of IIT Mandi
review_text:
Pros/Cons: That's a new IIT so infrastructure is very good . It is better as compared to old IIT It present in Himachal Pradesh so campus is very beautiful as it is in mountains. Robotics environment is very good , I can say it is better than some tier 2 collges Campus is located far from main airport city chandigarh , so it is hard to travel there Campus is small I think as it is 2nd generation IIT so it is in construction phase In Rainy season it is difficult to travel the nearest city due to flood and landslide situations . | Course Curriculum Overview: The curriculum is quite good . It is very relevant like we have course on thermodynamics, rigid body mechanics which is useful in mechanical engineering and there are also robotics course for all branches so that every one can know the basics of robotics . There are coding courses for everyone so that everyone can know tech field 
Also there are extra curricular courses which are very relevant to our daily life like Language speaking courses like french , German , english .
Also our college has started have semester exchange facility available to foreign colleges.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Mandi
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The study pressure is not too much like other IIT's. Availability of buses at proper time for travel between North amd South campuses and even to Mandi. Seniors are friendly and provide proper guidance to newcomers. The quality of food in mess and canteen is a bit average. | Placement Experience: Placements stats are good for the college.
Average packages:
CSE : 30Lpa
DSE : 25Lpa
Electrical : 20Lpa
Mechanical : 18Lpa
As we can see, these all the stats of average packages of some well known branches ans these are good enough for a fresh placement.
But currently due to recession, most of the students are unable to get placements and hence need to apply off campus to secure their future. With time, getting placements is getting much morw difficult.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM], Mandi
review_title: EACH AND EVERY DETAILS ABOUT IIT MANDI
review_text:
Pros/Cons: IIT Mandi is quite remote, but it uses this fact to its advantage. The calm you have around helps you to be more enrossed in your work. It is not just a college to the residents of the area, it is thier source of income. They simply love us and knowi ng that you are a major part of their community makes you stay here.Unlike what most people think that we scarcity of resources due to remote location, we have not. There is a school in campus itself. Anything can be brought here in a relatively quick time due well developed road transportation of HP. So faculty not feels as such.Branch change opportunities are very high. Just in case you realize that CSE is not your 'it', the lenient branch change rules help you a lot. You just need to A *developing* infrastructure, which cannot be compared to the present facilities at the old IITs. IIT Mandi is 15 kms away from the main Mandi city, so you get that Dominoz, and partying, and all that stuff at a stake of travelling 45minutes on bus (IIT has its own buses running every hour between Mandi and the Campus). So if you are too much into city life and stuff then you may not like IIT Mandi. The inter-college fests they organize are less attractive than the big colleges however you get the chance to contribute to the fest in a way or other.I do not have much idea about other branches, but for mechanical students there nothing much to learn about.They are not doing anything new. What they mostly do is mug up some researchpapers and | Course Curriculum Overview: Curriculum InfluencesInnovative Course Structure: The curriculum is designed to integrate technical education with humanities and social sciences, ensuring a well-rounded education.
Entrepreneurship Focus: The presence of the IIT Mandi Catalyst, a technology business incubator, encourages students to develop entrepreneurial skills alongside their technical education.
Broader Elective Options: Some students feel that offering a wider range of elective courses would allow for more personalized and diverse learning experiences.
Enhanced Practical Exposure: Increasing the number of industry visits, internships, and practical workshops could further bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and real-world applications Faculty Training: While the faculty is highly qualified, regular professional development and training sessions could help them stay updated with the latest teaching methodologies and technological advancements
