review_title: An In-depth review of IIT Tirupati
review_text: Pros/Cons: Clubs and extracurriculars, sports facilities Campus environment during winters is good. Some faculty are very helpful and friendly. Placement and internship drives are not up to the mark College community and culture is sometimes toxic Very limited alumni networking is there. | Placement Experience: There is a dedicated placement drive for students for campus placements. However, due to the recession these days, the process is a little dull. Otherwise, I expect good companies to take part in placement drives in IITs. Ultimately, the college profile plays a major role in the type of company coming to the college for placements. Students in the 7th semester become eligible for campus placements. I think the average package is around 12L. A minimum of 50 percent definitely gets placed. All the stats for these placements can be easily found on the college website.

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP], Tirupati
review_text:
Pros/Cons: IIT Tirupati boasts excellent placement services with a high percentage of students securing jobs in reputed companies. The placement cell offers excellent support for career advancement, including resume-building workshops, mock interviews The professors at IIT Tirupati are highly knowledgeable and bring a wealth of experience to their teaching. The administrative processes can sometimes be slow and cumbersome, leading to delays and frustrations in getting things done. Although the campus facilities are generally good, some areas still need improvement. | Placement Experience: Student has to do his internship after his 3rd year. It is mandatory. Companies come and hire student for Intern role later they offer PPO based on student performance
Yealry 20-30 companies come and make 50-60 offers
Accenture labs provide highest package of 42L and average pacge is aroung 21L
This year the placement percentage is around 80
Almost 50 students got placements 
I wanted to do a Job after my Btech for some years to get some industrial experience later i will think about further studies

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP], Tirupati
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Less restrictions: There is no such thing as intime and out time for hostels. If the students are responsible enough, this rule is a big plus when it comes to studying purposes. Supportive faculty: The faculty are very friendly and are available for discussions. Their knowledge in the subjects is very deep. Mess food: The quality of the food being served is never good. | Placement Experience: The placement process starts from the 7th semester. The placements in the college during the recession period were not very good for core as well. Most of the students from my department are unplaced. A lot of us are going for higher education. Just to make the numbers look good, the Placement Officer is bringing any and every company. None of us are placed in core jobs. One person bagged a software job, a few got into institutes like Allen. 

During the non recession period, most of the seniors got placed well. For students who choose to sit for software jobs from the core departments, well there are a good share of companies which are open for core branches as well. Develop good projects and you will be mostly able to get a software job.

Coming to internships, the internship season starts from 5th semester. Good number of companies come for internships. Expect low to no stipend in core and a you are good to go when it comes to core internships.

college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Bhilai
review_title: A good college with lots of upcoming developments in the future...
review_text: Pros/Cons: Being an IIT the college provides very good faculty and infrastructure. the faculty here are masters of their field all from prestigious institutes from all over world. They all very supportive and always ready to help the students. We have a state of the art infrastructure. Hostels rooms have AC. The academic complex is fully air conditioned. We have a common mess for boys and girls and the food quality is also good if compared to other government colleges. The crowd here is amazing. There are students here from almost all parts of the country. All are very hard working and supportive too. We get to learn a lot about their culture and their experiences. The gender ratio is 1:4 (boys:girls). This is not clearly good but improving over time. As we shifted to the permanent campus in 2022 so many facilities are still under in process. Especially the sports part. Currently it is not up to the mark but improving a lot over time. Sometimes it become quite difficult to handle the academics and the versatile extra curriculum activities simultaneously but it can be handled with proper time table and management. | Placement Experience: Students here are provided with internships and placements opprotunities in various core and non core sectors. Placements are provided in consulting, IT, technical, finance, analytics, management, teaching and research sectors. Highest and average packages offered were 48 and 13 lacks respectively. Major companies are Accenture Japan, Deloitte, Winjit, siemens, nvidia, tesco, mahindra and mahindra, tata autocomp, HCL, TCS, L&T ,etc. After BTECH many students opt for higher studies.

college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB], Bhilai
review_title: Regards: A student of IIT Bhilai
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The environment her is very amazing.You enjoy diversity here,you see and meet people from different regions which different cultures,practices and different languages because IITs are national platforms where you find students across India. You get a lot of opportunities to grow.You get opportunities to work on projects under professors in your interested field.You get research papers to work on if you are interested.You also form hackathon groups with most eligible candidates here. Not only studies,you find the other side i.e., the entertaining side also very amazing.Generally IITs have a lot of clubs like Art,Dance,Singing,Drama and many, and you get chance to enjoy that part too.Also you find great sports facilities,InterIIT. Mess food.Students here really need some good quality and non repetitive food.Mess always serves same curries a lot of time and we are fed up of this. As IIT Bhilai is a 3rd gen IIT,the sports facilities here are not up to the mark.They are well enough but need much more improvement.Some sports like football,Athletics don't have their own ground and the players go out of the campus for the practice Placements.As still there is some recession effect,you can see some decrease in the number of placement opportunities and thus students are suffering a little but this is the same scenario in many colleges ig. | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for placements in 4th year 1st sem itself and for that students have to have some given minimum cgpa and least given number of active backlogs.
We do get companies for placements from different places and domains.
We get placement offers from Luminous, Hindustan petroleum,ICICI,Nvdia,I'm beside you etc.
Highest package was 60 lakhs per annum and 16 lakhs is the average package going on.
Approx 50 to 65% of the batch gets places by the end of the placements procedure.
Once my degree is done,either I would work in a company or would go for further studies of MBA.

college_name: IIT Dharwad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITD], Dharwad
review_title: Life at IIT Dharwad - What to expect
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is really peaceful and away from the big city noise and chaos so it is good for studies and focus. The professors here are very helpful if you reach out. They answer all queries and needs peacefully and respectfully. And if you are into research you can also reach out to them and do your research work under their guidance and mentorship. The student community here is very active and well connected because it is a new campus and batch size is small, you'll probably remember all your batchmates both by their names and faces. The only thing I feel could be better is the core branch placements, they still lag behind CSE, so you need to put some extra effort during placements if you are from core branches | Placement Experience: At IIT Dharwad, students become eligible for campus placements right after their 7th semester(i.e. after the start of 4th year) and during their 3rd year, students also have opportunities to sit for internships which can later convert to PPOs.
The companies that have visited our campus are Adobe, Amazon, Texas, TCS, wipro etc. The highest CTC offered was 55LPA and average package for CSE is 18-20LPA, and for the core branches is around 10-15LPA.
In CSE more than 93% of the students got placed and many of other students opted for higher studies .
In the core branches the placement percentage was around 85-88%.
After My degree, I plan to go for a Software Engineer role.

college_name: IIT Bhubaneswar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITBBS], Bhubaneswar
review_title: Newbie review ( I spent only 6 months over here)
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is very clean and green. There are hills which create a pleasant climate. We could often go for trecking and beach is also not too far say 30 km. City is also close so that we don't need to spend much on autos.Teachers are friendly and didn't even expect that kind of behaviour from those experienced faculty. Curriculum is also very decent for all branches and cources. Language us a problem always. People speak less hindi. Everyone outside speaks more odia. Welcome to Gboard clipboard, any text you copy will be saved here.Welcome to Gboard clipboard, any text you copy will be saved here. Gutka is also a problem. Mess workers other people in campus use gutka. Few professors are rude towards students with their own rules. Distance from hostels to schools is more. We can take buses but they are often crowded. Girls to boys ratio is 1:5. | Placement Experience: Generally placements here start from the end of third year. That will go on till the fourth year.Amazon, google often visit for placements. Other companies like wipro, Federal also visit. Highest package of 2024 was 52 LPA. Overall highest package was about 60 LPA. In CSE around 96%studentes got placed and overall 89% students applied for placements have placed.

college_name: IIT Bhubaneswar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITBBS], Bhubaneswar
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Boys to girls ratio is around 4:1 and college atmosphere is overall good U get many opportunities to improve your skills and basic amenities are provided Faculty is good and curriculum is not that hectic Many areas are still under construction they should speed up for sure Curriculum has some subjects which are soo boring May be the gender ratio should be improved | Placement Experience: College placements are going pretty well, many of my seniors got placed into different companies, from 3rd year campus provides you internship and placement opportunities but u can also try for other remote placements 
Almost 95% of students of computer science branch gets placed every year

college_name: NIT Trichy, Tiruchirappalli
review_title: Your Concise review and guide to nit trichy
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The expansive green campus which seems to be never-ending, logos and orion lecture hall complexes are so fabulous, the placement cell building Capstone are some of the things i liked about my collegee The faculty are very friendly and helpful and you can clear  almost any of your  doubt with them, the labs are well-equipped as well and lab incharge are very knowledgeable as well The late night open shops like coke station, chaat khazana,sara food, etc are perfect for those late night cravings and that elaichi chai of coke station is something that has a special place in my heart. The mess here are not that good, like you'd expect a good mess for a college with a title of No.1 NIT but the messes are just subpar at best, the food quality is okayish and cleanliness is at an acceptable leve; the weather is almost sunny here so you can imagine how hard and frustrating it feels to cycle to lecture hall complexes and labs in afternoon when even going outside feels like you are being roasted alive. The  course often do not make sense and as a  personal development course there's only an english course, lack of other language course in first year is something i disliked | Placement Experience: The students start sitting for placement from 6th semester i.e the end of third year itself, while many students start getting job offers at the start of 7th semester itself.
The placememt percentage here is very good it is around 90% amd the average placement for 2025 placement session is around 15.76 lakh or maybe higher since results are not made available to us ye, the highest offer is around 55lakh+ according to some fourth year students.
The number of companies visiting us are many; they include tech giants such as google,microsoft,amazon, sony, samsung, NVIDIA, quolcomm, AMD, Yahoo and highliting companies are Ceat, Volvo, Wolkswagen, LT, Bosch, mcafee and the list goes on and on and on.
i plan on getting a job and then going for higher studies in a foreign university.

college_name: NIT Trichy, Tiruchirappalli
review_text:

Everything is student run, so students get several opportunities to work in event management, planning, logistics, tech, art, music, or anything that they are interested in. Strong alumni network across the world that holds regular meetups. Decent lab facility and 100% placement record across all departments. Extremely sexist and rigid administration Poor facilities in the hostel and departments Hostel administration, especially for girls, is hostile and unapproachable. Sexist curfew rules only for girls. | Placement Experience: Students sit for placements from the seventh semester. Companies like Google, Microsoft, Qualcomm, Texas Instruments, SAP Labs, AMD, IBM, Goldman Sachs, Adobe, Citi, Visa, Warner Bros, Amazon, Rubrik, Meesho, P&G, ITC, etc visit. 
Nearly 100% placement across all departments. 
For CSE, highest package was 40 LPA, average was 10 LPA
I plant to work after getting the degree

college_name: NIT Trichy, Tiruchirappalli
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus is huge we can really explore the campus it has been more than one year in the campus i still haven't seen my entire campus its good to have a big campus The placements of this college is very nice for all the branches the average package of all the branches is very very good and far above than other colleges The peer group of this college is very good since it is the top NIT of India there are a lot of smart people here which improves our own charachter and iq. This college provides a very good placements both on and off campus to its students the placement percentage is also very good for all the branches ofcourse the placement packages are higher for students of CSE AND ECE branches but others also get a very good package. Students get eligible for placements from the 4th year some even get a PPO from the company they do their intership at in the third year. Big and Prominent comapnies visit our college such as Google, Microsoft, Samsung, Tata Steel, Qualcom, NVIDIA and more recently they gave around 900 offers to the students the highest package was aroung 65 lpa and the average package stood at 16 lpa for the college which is very good as compared to other colleges. The student placement percentage is also very good where aroung 85-90 percent of the students gets placed evey year in defferent sectors like software, Data Science, Management and Core sectors of their branch.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITK], Surathkal
review_title: A mini heaven on earth called NITK
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus is of approx 300 acres, lush green and has its own private beach, the NITK beach. Lab facilities are decent, we have Central Research Facility were all sorts of advanced machines and softwares are there. Faculties are good. Placements are really good. All top MNCs come to campus for recruitment. Highest CTC can go upto 60 lpa. Some faculties may not be very good with their teaching method Overall infrastructure is decent but it can be better especially the hostel rooms. Resolving some basic issues like fan, tubelight water supply takes a lot of time. | Course Curriculum Overview: I chose Mtech Thermal Engineering because I was interested in thermal subjects like Fluid mechanics, Heat transfer, CFD. The Curriculum is pretty decent and it does covers the fundamentals and advanced topics of the particular subject but it could be better in terms of imparting practical and industrial knowledge. The exams that are held in NITK are competitive and requires a good understanding of the subject. You simply cannot cram and get good marks in exam. You have to know the proper implementation of that particular formula or theory.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITK], Surathkal
review_title: nitk- a college you can go for
review_text: Pros/Cons: campus is vast, lush,green, climate is hot. There is bus service, infrastructure is good and a new lecture hall is almost completed. college provides opportunities for placement and internships. there is a decently equipped gym, proper tennis court, swimming pool which has been opened recently, football ground (not turf) u can also play cricket, there is volleyball area and 2 basketball court. an indoor badminton court is also there there is ample options for snacks, night canteens, cafeteria. they are at resonable rates.hostels are good. a new hostel building has been built which provides hot water for bath as well as better funitures for students departments such as Information Technology, lack proper professors. There is a nice canteen called Fodd court and it is veg (if it was non veg it wouldve been good) since there is a large number of students some places can be very crowded. we have faced water issues (beggining of the year usually) as well as power cuts . they are not very frequent  but affect life boys- girl ratio is poor (10:2 imo). students are way too competitive. | Placement Experience: there is the CDC (career development centre) that handles placements. they invite companies for recruitment. notable companies are Google, Uber, Nvidia, Texas insturments, Bosch, goldman sachs, oracle. most companies take 1-10 people based on their needs. there are usually 2 rounds (skill based test and HR interviews). around 90% of Cs students get placed. Texas instruments gives a CTC of 38 lakhs(can vary based on roll and year). I am trying to get placements and land a role in an electronics company such as Nvidia , Texas, ARM, Qualcomm . having a good CGPA is very helpful, there is the student build college site (IRIS) which provides guides and reviews from seniors about each companies and their exams, how they prepared for it etc. placement season is a hectic time where u r tensed till u get a company. Due to the current economic and political situation of the world the placements have been affected.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Rourkela
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is huge, green and well equipped with great central library, good laboratoried and active student club. Hostel life and campus activities under SAC made my 5 years socially engaging. Strong faculty with good subject knowledge and supportive mentorship. excellend library and research facilities for chemistry. Vibrant cultural life: college fests, clubs, sports and a healthy peer learning environment. Gender ratio is highly skewed: roughly 1:4 or 1:5 For pure science branches placement opportunities are limited as compared to engineering students, students must put in extra efforts for industrial roles. Academic content for science students in bsc is not as par with with the student of other proper dedicated universities for science. when the student join in msc andthe classes are merged with int msc student the difference in knowledge can be seen. | Placement Experience: Placements are definitely there for coaching centres in my branch. Many big and reputed coaching institutions like akash, byjus etc came for recruitment. 
More R & D comapnis can be included. the nterview process and the packages were decent for the role offered.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Rourkela
review_text:

The academic schedule is so effective that we get a lot of free time so that we can boost our skills in self learning and skill development,also ther are two long vacations summer for around 3 months and winter for 1month which is so good The transportation inside the campus, all are said to use cycles of course they are good for health but a longer time we might get bored of cycling There are some professors who are very much qualified but they lack in methods of teaching which quite upsets me , also some lack in language also ,the main language used locally is hindi but the classes are taken in English but in an Indian standard There are no good South Indian restaurants inside and outside the campus | Placement Experience: From the 6th or 7th sem one can go for pre placement or internship their cgpa and skills are measured for this.

There are a lot of good companies coming for placements in our college , companies like GOOGLE , AMD , Qualcomm, Amazon, oracle, ola , microsoft , apple etc comes with a higher offers
There was a total of 1274 placement offers this year.


The highest package of this year was 62.44 LPA
and average package was 14-15 LPA

The placement rate of Btech program was about 82.20 percentage .

Also this year our college up ranked from 19 to 13th in engineering becoming the second best NIT in India

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Rourkela
review_title: Honest and Genuine review of NIT Rourkela
review_text:
Pros/Cons: So, the Positive aspects of the college are that big companies come for the place Like Amazon, NVIDIA, Tata, Texas Instruments and many more. And also, the fees is relatively less than the other engineering colleges. And also many workshops happens. The college life is decent and also the quality of social Interaction is good in this college as people from different regions of India comes here. Some faculties are very interested in teaching but some are not very much interested . The quality of infrastructure in this college is not so good as this college made in 1960s, But new buildings are also on constructions and the amenities that the campus provides is the all sports access and also olympic level pool is also available. There are no Almirah's in the hostel and also the Internet speed is decent and the connectivity for the NIT Rourkela is not so good And the power cuts are very frequent and there is also no generator available for this. The curriculum is not up to date The college is only focusing on theory but not on the skills for the students and therefore students have to work by their own to develop such skills which require for the placements. Also the mess food Is very decent and some lecture rooms are also not well maintained as there are issues of the eco of the voice of professor in the lecture halls. | Placement Experience: So the students become eligible for placemant usually from the 7th semester of the Btech which is from the 4th year and the internship opportunities are available from the 3rd year onwards that is from 5th and 6th semester.
So in year 2024-25 the number of companies that visited The campus is around 330 And the total job offers offered is about 1200.
So the highest package in the EC branch is about 1.2 crore per annum and the average package for the EC branch is around 13 to 14 lakhs.
And the Profile of jobs are core electronics, VLSI and semiconductor roles ,system and embedded developments, software engineering, IT services, consulting and analytics.
The percentage of student which are getting placed are in the range of 90 to 99% for ECE branch.
The top recruiters are Apple Google, Microsoft, Amazon, wipro, Deloitte, Tata,Vedanta etc.
After completing my degree in ECE from NIT Rourkela my plan is to join the core technology sector as a VLSI and semiconductor engineer in a reputed multinational Companies, leveraging the strong foundation and industrial exposure gained during my studies in case I do not secure a campus placement I aim to pursue the off campus opportunities in electronics, IT or analytics sectors. Alternatively I am open to perceive higher studies in MBA to specialize in the management sector.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Rourkela
review_text: Pros/Cons: I really liked the infrastructure and the facilities provided by the college.The classrooms big and all classrooms have AC. Cleanliness is maintained very nicely across the whole campus. Student are only allowed to use cycles, Pollution is controlled Inside the campus there are lot of shops , restraunts, stalls etc so students don't have to go outside the campus to buy anything. Almost every hostels has stationary shops and printout facilities so it is very convinient. The fests and the events are a major part of the college. There are many clubs and students can join them according to their interests. This helps in overall development of the students and aloow us to meet people and make friends. There is a 85% attendance in NIT Rourkela and it is strict. So we have to attend the classes even if we don't like some of them.Skipping classes can result in penalty. I think it should be 75% like other colleges. The first year hostel rooms are a little bit small compared to other hostels' rooms so it feels a little congested.The top floors of hostels are hot compared to other floors so buyin a coller is very much necessary for he students in top floors. There are some subjects that are not related to the branch but we have to study them for grades so i think those subjects should be removed.The subjects should be related to the branch. | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for campus placements in there fourth year of college from 1st semester. The average package is around 12 lakh and around 90-95 % of students are places in all branches. The top recruiters of metallurgy branch include Jindal , TATA Steel and JSW. The highest package was around 1.2 cr and it was from ece branch. Over 342 companies visited NIT Rourkela and around 1300+ job offers were made. I plan on doing a job probably and i dont plan to do higher studies.

review_title: “Life and Learning at MNIT Jaipur: An Insider’s Perspective”
review_text:

Eligibility for Campus Placements

Students typically become eligible for the main campus placement drive from the 7th semester (final year).

Companies set specific academic eligibility criteria, which often include:

* A minimum B.Tech CGPA of around 7.0

Pre-Placement Offers (PPOs) can be secured through excellent performance during the summer internship period, usually after the 6th semester.

Specific company data for Biotechnology is generally combined with overall placement statistics. Prominent recruiters often include TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Deloitte, and Capgemini, along with some core sector companies.

Biotechnology students are commonly offered roles in IT/Software, Data Analytics, and Consulting, in addition to core R&D and Pharma profiles.

3. Highest and Average Package Details

Package statistics vary from year to year:

* Highest Package (2023–24): Approximately ?11.89 LPA
* Average Package (2023–24): Approximately ?6.91 LPA
* Average Package (2022–23): Approximately ?9.02 LPA

The highest packages are generally offered by IT and analytics firms to students with strong coding and quantitative skills.


4. Percentage of Students Getting Placements

The placement percentage for the Biotechnology branch has shown significant fluctuation:

* 2022–23 Placement Rate: Approximately 90.32%
* 2023–24 Placement Rate: Approximately 30.19%

Placement percentages vary depending on the number of eligible students and overall market conditions each year.

review_text:
Pros/Cons: Infrastructure & Campus Environment  Pretty good infrastructure: labs, classrooms are well equipped. Smart classrooms in many places. 2. Faculty / Teaching / Mentorship  Many students say faculty are experienced, knowledgeable, helpful and fairly approachable. Mentorship from seniors and professors is decent in many branches. 3. Campus Life & Social Interaction  Students seem to enjoy campus life: clubs, fests, cultural / technical events are active. 1. Curriculum & Relevance  Some feel that though updates are done, many what is taught is theoretical and not always well aligned with what industry expects. Infrastructure / Maintenance Issues  Some hostels (especially second year or later) have issues: cleanliness, AC / ventilation, old buildings needing renovation. Mess / food quality is often criticized. 3. Placement Variability Based on Branch  Non-core branches (e.g. Civil, Biotech) are said to have fewer top offers, fewer companies coming, lower average packages. | Placement Experience: Strengths & Weaknesses in Placement

Strengths

Good number of companies participate; wide variety (IT & non-IT).

Strong average & top packages in popular branches (CSE, ECE, EE).

Reasonable placement rate ~80-85% for many branches.


Challenges / Weaknesses

For less in-demand / non-IT / non-core branches, average packages are lower. (E.g. Civil, Mechanical have much lower averages in recent drives) 

Also, in some years (like 2024), placement rate dipped (to ~72%) perhaps due to wider economic trends. 

Students who have backlogs, weak intern/project experience, or who are not proactive often struggle or get fewer offers.

review_text: Pros/Cons: I liked the pleasant environment here with more trees More trees and ponds in the campus with campus area 650 acres Supportive staff and seniors help in the studies Hostel maintainance is poor as they are some times dirty Bathrooms are some times not cleaned often Classes are small and in summer very hot too | Placement Experience: Yeah placements are decent in the college with companies like google, amazon,tata, infosys and wipro like companies are offering jobs with a good packages with in the college it self and these placements average package is good

college_name: NIT Silchar, Silchar
review_title: Nit Silchar the beautiful college to visit
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is really good and it is very big. Seniors are really helpful. Well connected to outside as Train station and airport is very near. Professors are highly qualified and highly expert in their respective fields. The food is average in the hostel mainly 1st year hostel. There is lot of network issue and mainly we depend on the LAN connection. Some classroom are very old and need to upgrade. | Placement Experience: The placements are really good here top companies visit every year offer lot of packages. The placements generally starts from 7th semester and continue till end of 8th semester. 
Mainly the highest package is offered by tech companies and last year it was about 52 LPA. 
The average package is about 14LPA for Electronics and Instrumentation branch. And 52 LPA is highest. 
Almost 85-90% students get placed.

college_name: NIT Silchar, Silchar
review_text: Pros/Cons: The natural environment outside. With lots of trees and natural beauty around it gives a soothing study environment The academic environment is great with balanced fests and exams schedules and time for self introspection and developing skills Various clubs designed and students can join as per their interest Class timings. The class duration is from Monday to Friday, morning 8 to 5 in the evening. And waking up at 8 on a coldy weather is not pleasable. Too much orientation from clubs lined up all together. | Placement Experience: From 6th semester students are offered various internship opportunities and placements opportunities start afterhand from the onset of 8th semester. Last year in 2024, the placement record stood at the highest pachage offered 52.89LPA and an average of 13.54LPA. More than 200 companies visited and more than 600 offers were made.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text:

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Nit hamirpur campus is very beautiful. Also ground is very big . Faculties are very good and supportive . Placements are very good and one of the best for core branches with 90+ placement rate even the crore branches placement rate are 85+. Sports facility and gym facilities are in each hostels There is water problem durning the raining season but faculty are working on it There are monkeys in the campus generally they aren't harmful but sometimes the may harm. The campus is too big so People who walk less may have problem | Placement Experience: At NIT Hamirpur, students become eligible for campus placements from the 7th semester onwards, and the placement scenario has been quite encouraging in recent years. A wide range of companies such as Infosys, Wipro, L\&T, Capgemini, Tata Projects, and several core as well as IT firms visit the campus every year. The highest placement touched an impressive 2.05 crore offered to a mechanical engineering student, while the overall average package stands at around 10.5 LPA, which is a strong figure compared to many other NITs. The placement percentage remains high, with a majority of students securing offers, especially in branches like CSE, IT, and ECE, though core branches like civil and mechanical also receive good opportunities. The profiles offered include roles in software development, consulting, design, project engineering, and research. After completing my degree, I plan to take advantage of these placement opportunities to gain solid industry experience and later decide whether to pursue higher studies or competitive exams for broader career growth.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_title: The most beautiful college  NIT Hamirpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: It has one of the most beautiful campus and it it is surrounded by greenery all around. There is 95% placement in mechanical engineering which is my department. We have grand fests like hillfair which is cultural fest and nimbus which is technical . We have various cultural and technical clubs and societies which we can join according to our interest and we can join it by giving interviews taken by our seniors.Apart for them we have sports club ,gym with various equipments Hostel food is good ,we get all the basic nutrients required in hostel and four meels is there. Apart from them we have good food shops in campus like verka , Amul ,pooja shopping centre ,food court ,food plaza There is a lot of restrictions to girls . Intiming is 6:30 for the first year and 9:30 for the rest . And no outings are allowed at the initial period in campus There is road connectivity issue as airport as well as railway station are very from college and there is very small market nearby Many people here fall in drug abuse and even the professors are very strict and not student friendly | Placement Experience: Placement rate is good in mechanical engineering which is Almost 90 percent and average package is 6-8 lakh . From seventh semester students become eligible for college placement. And many good companies visit like Jsw ,adani ,realiance and many more.
Highest package is 1 crore but it was in it sector.
After getting degree I will opt for placements

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_title: A journey of excellence in mountains :NIT HAMIRPUR
review_text: Pros/Cons: infrastructure of college is very good have many facilities in NIT hamirpur like gym , student activity centre , almost all kind sports , big campus and every required facility is there for betterment of the individual . Lush green campus , flowers and gardens inside the campus . Big grounds and newly made SAC student activity centre If campus is big sometimes you have walk a long way you dont find dominos , mcdonalds , big bazaar like big brands Hamirpur connectivity is not by railways or airport | Placement Experience: College placement are good , very high for IT branches that is CSE and ECE having packages sometimes higher than 50LPA but it also offer decent placement to core branches with average package of 6LPA.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_title: I have very good experienced for reviewing here., I hope this helped to others for future admission.
review_text:

Hostel food sometime is not good but after our complain they resolve this issue. In Hostel students seat is fixed so some of the students not getting any place if they are late. | Placement Experience: The placement of this college is superb. 
the students were selected in the 7th to 8th  semester.
In my time the company visited is 35.
The percentage of the students selected is approx 99%.
I am planning to work for 2 years after that I resign from the job and going for higher education.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Environment as campus have alot of greenery around it and alot of hills and goof places to visit Infrastructure is really good and beautiful alot of good places to spend time  | Placement Experience: Placement is okay around 10 lakh per annum is average.  Placements in cse is best and almost all the students get placed. And from many other branches students are well placed so i can say our college has a decent placements

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: I like the fact that professors teach relatively better than degree college I also like the infrastructure of the institute I like the sporta event that takes place I dont like the quality of food that is provided in the mess Also  the room size is relatively  small I also dont like the fact the online food delivery is limited to campus gate | Course Curriculum Overview: I think that the course curriculum in terms of relevance and comprehensiveness is good after first year. But in the first year a student has to study the basic subjects of all the branchs which is not needed

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus is amazing and is very big, hostels and lecture halls surrounded by literal jungles. It also has amazing views from different locations of Triund Mountain range. The alumni network is very strong and the overall college culture between students of different years is amazing. It is a place where you grow up from a kid to a mature person. The campus is pretty neat and clean, it's hard to find litter on roads and ground. The campus also has most of facilities regarding sports. The college board is not much of a listener to a lot of problems and it's getting stricter day by day. The professors are not the best, you might find great professors at NIT Hamirpur but most of them are just the typical government job employees trying to pass time | Placement Experience: The students become eligible for placements when they start final year. On an average there's more than 100 companies that come for placements and they almost hire 90% of the batch. The highest package I have heard was 30LPA for software engineering role.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: The infrastructure and view of college is so pleasing, I would say one of best campus in India The library is so vast to have all ranges of books Promotes cultural activites through it's clubs and societies Branch Upgradation is not possible now from one branch to higher demanded branch Norms regarding short attendance should be changed | Placement Experience: In this time when most of colleges are losing companies to reach and many students are remaining unemployed companies are still reaching our institute . Collge runs a Startup internships program in which many startup companies reach our college and pick students for paid internships , College is also promoting startups by providing funds thorugh projects like E- Crescendo . On campus Plcements start from 3rd year . Highest package was from my own branch Dual Cse that was a year before that was 1.73 crores per annum. Average package reported is currently 18 lakhs per annum. In mechanical branch it was 100 % placement rate. overall it was around 70 % placement rate . My plans after getting degree is to get a job experience then start my own startup idea. compsnies visiting were accenture , infosys , godrej , samsung , capgemini , ibm etc

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Hamirpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Beautiful campus with lush greenary around People from different parts of india are here..some foreigners also Placement for computer science people are really good Almost no placement for architecture students | Course Curriculum Overview: My reason to choose this course was keenly dependent  on my interest n passion for building and constructing newer form . Faculty is amazing in what they do and teach . Also india is in its intial stages to accept architecture.that's one of reason for less placement anywhere..be it here or any other college

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Strong placement records with is strong alumni network Lots of extra curricular activities which lots of experience for industries Single hostel rooms with Internet facilities As college is made in 1960 infrastructure are little old The infrastructure of labs are old but new equipments are there It has vast campus area but most of land is covered with forest | Course Curriculum Overview: The curriculum exam ready for students of mechanical engineering it focuses on industrial level exposure and for other competitive exams like gate and IES with good faculty who teaches almost all concepts. There are labs for each subject in which we get real world application knowledge. Also course is updated regularly as per the requirement of 
 industry . The course can we improved by making course more practical rather than a writing all things on practical it should include project for students. 
Frequency of exam is that in every 15 days there is a exam with moderate to difficult level.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The academic block of college was newly build with centralized ac in every classroom. Class rooms are neat and able to accommodate up to 120 members in the class room. New hostel rooms will be provided for first years with single room for each. Clubs for popular sports available in the campus. Poor food quality , mostly the foods available are in north style. South style food was very rare here. But good food for North Indians would be available. Wi-fi problem was severe in the campus. Many complaints raised about it. | Placement Experience: 1.The main thing about our campus, campus placements are so good. Branches like CSE & ECE have high placement percentage about 90.
2.From 3rd year students got involved college internships and programmes that allow them to better placements.
3.Top companies like Google,Microsoft,Amazon,Intel,Samsung etc visited our campus. And the highest package of our campus is about 60LPA offered by Amazon.
4.I planned to work after completing my B.Tech.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_title: Very much Honest feedback. It took me 30 minutes.
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Placements of core branches are also very good as compared to other NITs/some IITs. Lush green campus, trees are planted very much. Some professors are really very very good. They are of high calibre. Some professors are very bad in character too. Corruption is all when I see the poor development of infrastructure. Inequality in branches can be seen often. | Placement Experience: The students usually get offers more than 15 lpa and highest this year one guy from ECE got into rubrics of 1.23 crores and many of the students from electronics branch and computer science and branch got package of 30 Lpa 48 lpa 60 lpa too,
and coming to the core branches, scenarios are very much good in core branch, I think many people almost everybody get placed here and average package of core branches 10 lpa and of non core it must be 15 to 20 lpa so over all average package of college must be approx 15 lpa.

In my branch metallurgy and material science engineering, every student got placed.

Internships are given generally from 3rd year itself but many of my friends got internship in 2nd year itself.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: College infrastructure including academic buildings, library and computer center. Strong teaching pedagogy with highly qualified professors A strong sporting and extra curricular culture. Lack of internships opportunities provided by college The placements are not in par with other NITs//IITS | Placement Experience: Students get PPO ( pre placement opportunity) from 5th semester itself. However the actual placment process starts from 7th semester. A mix of tech and non-tech companies visit the college and offer job to the students.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The college has quite a good academic building but the hostels are old and not very well maintained The crowd is nice and quite competitive, people are smart and quite sharp The professors are a bit on a stricter side however some are quite chill, they might make you work hard in order to score good Hostel and mess food is not up to date, often we face issues like insects in mess food or stale vegetables being served. | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for internship after 4th semester , after 6th semester the companies start coming for regular placement 
For cse the average was around 20 lpa with median being 16-17 lpa 
Companies that generally visit are - atlassian, Amazon, flipkart , BNY Mellon, cisco, Texas Instruments etc
Around 140 offers were made and in a batch of 120, 100 have secured placement 
Placement percentage is around 92% 
Highest package - 65 lpa atlassian (on campus)
1.23 cr rubrik (off campus)
Lowest package - 7 lpa
Average - 18 - 20 lpa

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_text: Pros/Cons: Best academic environment across all NITs Best infrastructures and supportive seniors Placement of the college is excellent and average package is getting increased day to day. Classes scheduled are tough and it is difficult to manage. Distance of the Hostels and classroom are slightly far. Mess food quality are not too good  and I should be improved | Placement Experience: Placement of the college is very impressive almost 98% students were placed last year with the highest packege of 86 lakh and average of 14 lakh per annum.The placement percentage of my branch was 100% last year. Few of the World famous companies like Cisco, Google, Microsoft, Adobe, Atlassian etc come for the placement

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Jamshedpur
review_title: The Hidden Diamond : The NIT Jamshedpur
review_text:

The Hostels were too much conjusted and the building were too old and there is very less maintainence by the administration . There is very restricted participation in various events makes the first year student very disappointment. The Labs were too old and machines used were also too old , during the time of RIT JSR. | Course Curriculum Overview: The Course Provided by the Administration is sufficient and very relevent , but actually the Timings of the Classes and Labs might trouble you .
The Faculties were highly qualified and the way of their teaching is very effective (you might face issue in starting of the course but you become familiar with it soon)

The Special Cause for taking Mechanical Engineering is due to the high Placement percentage and its average ctc package . It also provides a huge scope into the Government Jobs as well as private sector .

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITP], Patna
review_title: Honest student review of NIT Patna.
review_text: Pros/Cons: I liked the placement like of mechanical engineering there is 100 percent placement. It is the best NIT for PHD or if you want to persue higher studies. There are a lot of resources. I love the collection in my library there are a lot of books on academics, religion, philosophy, editing or etc. The faculty are awesome they are chill and amazing. They will never scold you if you ask silly doubts with them. The first I disliked is the girl to boy ratiio like there is only 12 to 15 girls in my section where total students are 70. There is birds poop everywhere around campus. I have to always carry a umbrella with me. Patna campus size is only 34 acre and new patna bihata campus size is 125 acre. Civil and Mechanical studenents will be in patna campus and other will be in bihata campus within few years. so i disliked this discrimination. | Placement Experience: Placements are in my college starts from 7th semester. Core electrical engineering placements are limited, and mostly IT And specialisation Branches get more placement opportunities. The average package in my college is moderate and also depends on skill. Many students NIT Patna prefer the GATE examination and higher studies. If no campus placement is offered to me then I will plan to continue my family business and after getting my degree, I will join a good company and save money to invest in a good running business

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITP], Patna

review_title: Nit patna review from first year student.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITP], Patna
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The helping nature of seniors and the culture of tech seniors No ragging culture, that is zero ragging at all and seniors are very supportive The Student activity centre and the club's of NITP No fests , no freshers party at all. The 2024-29 batch have not experienced any single fest. Either technocultural or cultural. No disco night. There is no student council, so if students have any grievances there is no one to fulfill their problems. The labs are not having the proper arrangements and all the instruments. Example I am the student of chemical Technology but still there is no proper instruments in the lab. The Chemical Technology department is merged with chemistry that is a big. | Course Curriculum Overview: The course is not so good. The syllabus is old and has to be revised according to new chemical engineering syllabus. It is merged with MSC chemistry syllabus that is not relevant at all. 
The faculties do not teach very well. They are not clear with their concepts.  

The end sem exam comes very early after the mid sems that is a cons.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITP], Patna

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NITP], Patna

review_text: Pros/Cons: The placement ratio is quite good and getting better year by year. Some faculties are really well and some of them are even hitting top 2% of worlds scientists. college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Raipur
review_title: NIT Raipur Review: Where knowledge meets real world engineering.
review_text:
Pros/Cons: I liked the curriculum of this college most because of their involvement of cutting edge tools and technologies related subjects and availability of good quality lab equipment's in labs and well qualified faculties. . Apart from academics the placement cell of this college is too supportive and try to bring as much companies as possible including many public sectors companies and top of Multi national corporations. This college hold a good position in sports domain as a lot of students are doing great in the field of sports and getting top positions in Inter NITs  sports like cricket, kabaddi, and football etc.. The infrastructure of this college is a bit old and campus is even too small as compared to the campus of other NITs. Its campus only covers an area of around 125 acres that's quite low as comparing the average of all NITs average. This college is also lagging a bit in the sectors associated with research and development. Since this college has a lot of professors in top 2% scientists still fails to publish research papers. This college also disappoints me when it comes to provide supports to technical clubs like Motorsports, Aviation, Technocracy and robotics. So its difficult for students ti produce good results as compared to other colleges. | Placement Experience: 1. Students from seventt semester and onwards are eligible for the campus placements.
2. There are ample numbers of comapnies visited in our college like schneider electric, bpcl,bhel,bhel,reliance,catterpillar,eil, and so on. As per the last year data around 130 comapnies made in to our campus and they offered around 800 plus offers to various studenst of different departments.
3. The highets package offered this year is 60 lakhs that is offered by DE Shaw and average package is something around 11 laks per annum
4. Taking considerations of students of all departments, almost 70 percent students get placed.
5. Presently my plan after getting degree is to get into a job.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Raipur
review_title: Life of Master of Technology at NIT Raipur.
review_text:
Pros/Cons: I would like to appreciate the faculty members at the college. The faculty members are highly educated and help the students to learn and improve skills. Here the students are co-operative. If one in need, then the other person would help that person. The behavior is gentle. The seniors are helpful. They themself ask to come to them and gather knowledge and learn problem solving skills from them. The campus has limited hostels for boys and girls. So the students have to live outside the campus to live in the PG rooms or private hostels. The building is quite old and has not been modified since long time. One main building is there where different blocks are interconnected. The closing time of the hostel gate is early by 10 PM. So many students cannot walk in the night outside the campus. | Course Curriculum Overview: In my curriculum Information Technology, we have to study Cloud computing, Advanced Machine Learning, Advanced Data Structure and Analysis, Distributed System, Blockchain Technology, Computer Networking, Data Science, Bioinformatics and many more subjects are taught. I choose this course as because it is aligned with my interest and matches my Bachelor of Technology field.

By learning Cloud Computing, Advanced Data Structure Analysis, Advanced Machine Learning, Blockchain Technolgy; we get ready for industry job. In this course we learn how to solve real-world problems and solutions to them.

For improvement in the curriculum, I would suggest not to involve more theory rather than go for more practical learning.

The exams happens after every 3 months in a year. Generally the end-semester happens at December and May. The exam is moderate level.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], Agartala
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Beautiful, spacious campus with greenery Students often mention the campus is large, lush with plants, trees and well-maintained gardens. well-furnished and functional.   The library is praised for its collection, environment, usefulness.   Sports facilities are good; there’s a strong sports complex that students enjoy. Students appreciate the cultural and technical fests — Moksha (cultural) and Aayam (technical) are especially popular.   There are many clubs (technical, cultural) giving opportunities beyond academics. Being located in Tripura, it's seen as relatively remote; travelling to/from is harder or more costly.   Poor connectivity to major industrial hubs, which reduces chances of industrial visits/internships sometimes. Hot and humid weather for a large part of the year is uncomfortable.   Some concern about air quality, or general environmental upkeep (dust, “jungle” overgrowth, etc.) in parts of the campus. WiFi / internet service in hostels is weak or unreliable. Limited daily data caps (e.g. ~2 GB/day) are considered insufficient for many students’ needs. | Placement Experience: Placements in NIT Agartala are actually decent, especially for the top branches like CSE, IT, ECE, Mechanical, and Electrical. Every year many companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, L&T, Capgemini, Deloitte, Microsoft, etc. visit the campus. The average package is around 7–8 LPA for CSE/ECE, while core branches get slightly lower, and the highest goes up to 40+ LPA in some years.

For other branches like Civil, Chemical, and Production, the placements are a bit lower, but students still manage through campus plus off-campus drives. What I like is that the placement cell is active, and we also get internship opportunities in our 3rd year which help us prepare.

review_title: A very honest review of NIT SIKKIM
review_text:
Pros/Cons: There is very good environment in sikkim, mount kanchanjunga is clearly visible from hostel rooms . Very peaceful place for study . Most of professors are well qualified here . Faculties used to teach industry relevant things . So, despite being soo much problem here , students used to placed in big mncs from here like apple, nvedia, microsoft..etc Right now, Infrastructure here is not well, in hostels there is fixed timming for water supply. But there is new permanent campus is setting up in namdong , so, we are hoping for better . Some professors are literally so irritable here. They treat students like school going nursery kids . Academic pressure is soo much here, professors used to give soo much assignments, projects, copy paste stuffs ..etc, they used to take viva , class tests , presentation regularly(every week). There is continuous evaluation process. | Placement Experience: Placement is overall good here. Students from ece/eee are better placed than cse here.  Here, companies like nvedia, apple has given the highest package. Previous year highest package is 55 lakh ,which had given to two students from eee branch. This year highest package is 35 lakh . Which is offered by two students.
Now ,I am concluding all branch avg and placement percentage -
Civil -70%placed
Mechanical- 100% placed 
Eee-92%
Ece-90%
Cse/ai- 80%
Overall -86%
Average - 8.85 lpa
Median-7.5 lpa
Highest -35 lpa

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], South Sikkim
review_text:

Kanchenjunga (world's 3rd highest peak) is visible from campus of NIT Sikkim which is totally mesmerizing and breathtaking. Familiar behavior and nature of professors around you will treat you as their students and the professors are very much supportive in nature Infrastructure still needs to be improved a lot as students forced to line in a 5 bedded sharing rooms where it becomes very difficult for a student to follow their own routine and along with hostels the facilities are also not that top notch Course curriculum is very hectic as so many exams will be there including 2 mid terms and one major end term and more or less the professors focuses on theoretical things not on the practical applications. | Placement Experience: If you are capable enough you will be getting decent placements, Overall placement statistics lies about 80-90% with an average pacakage of 9-10 lacs per annum including 90-95% for CSE, ECE. EEE and almost 80-85% for core branches like Mechanical and Civil. Some of major past recruiters are CDAC, CDOT, BEL, VEDANTA, DELOITTE, MAHINDRA, MARUTI SUZUKI, UKG, GENPACT, BPCL, L&T, ALSTOM, NVIDIA, MICROSOFT, SLB, TERADATA, CGI and many others. Highest pacakage for the batch of 2023-24 was 44 LPA by NVIDIA and average was around 9.95 LPA.

From start of the 7th semesters , the students can register themselves for placements and from 5th semester onwards student can register themselves for internship opputunities.

NIT Sikkim also provide opportunities of highes studies like PhD from reputed institutes like IIT Hyderabad, IIT Delhi and some other prominent institutions as well.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], South Goa
review_text: Pros/Cons: NIT Goa is located in the dream vacation place of India and abroad. It is nice to visit peaceful beaches of goa to escape from hectic college life sometimes. Also Goa is not just beaches and parties, it is much more and we have seen all of it. NIT Goa inspite of being new NIT is growing at a very fast pace. Honestly when I joined the college i wasn't so happy but now the picture is really different, infrastructure, equipments, culture, every aspect is improving with each joining batch. Professors here are really supportive when it comes to your academics and except some professors most of them support personal development too. Also you get enough time for your side hustles if you are a student of NIT Goa. In timing of Nit Goa is 9 pm after that campus gates are closed. So we don't really get to enjoy the night life of Goa. Also from this year Institute is making it compulsory for everyone to stay in hostels, so PGs are not an option anymore. There are less clubs in NIT Goa and even the present clubs like ecell is not active. The recent joined batches have initiated many new clubs but because of less population clubs are not doing very well. Attendance is a big issue here specially if you are unlucky to get strict professors. Minimum attendance required here is 80% and we have to run to professors requesting them to let us give the exam in the last days of semesters. | Placement Experience: Final students are eligible for placements. Companies such as turtle mint, findem, Nokia, IFB, Samsung, Adani power, HDFC Bank, Tesco, Upskill, ONGC, Jio, and many more. Last year almost everyone got placed. Average package is 10 LPA. This year though placements were not as expected because of economic depression. Even after the placements, lay offs have become common. Hence to cope up with the competition we need to keep upskilling.

college_name: National Institute of Technology - [NIT], New Delhi
review_title: NIT Delhi: A Balanced Blend of Academics, Opportunities, and Campus Life
review_text: Pros/Cons: Sports facilities are very good, and ground is well maintained Mess food quality is not the best, curd is very thin | Placement Experience: The annual placements at NIT Delhi are consistently getting better year by year where the top branches such as CSE, ECE, and IT are leading the way with their strong placement rates. Many students get hired at very attractive salaries by well-established companies like TCS, Infosys, Cognizant, Microsoft, and Amazon. For example, the CTC of the best offers have reached as high as ?15–20 LPA in some instances. The Mechanical, Civil, and Electrical branches also contribute to the university’s overall placement record to some extent. Those students usually boost the available opportunities for themselves by taking up internships and off-campus offers. Moreover, the students who fail to get a job on the campus can still benefit out of the college atmosphere where the strong basics of coding, projects, and practical skills are taught which makes the students quite employable at startups, off-campus, or even when going for higher studies like M.Tech/GATE/abroad. To sum up, NIT Delhi is a very solid foundation for the students whether they want to have a career or continue with their education.

review_text:

college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design and Manufacturing - [IIITDM], Jabalpur
review_title: Life at IIITDM Jabalpur: Placements, Campus Experience, Hostels & Internship Opportunities
review_text:

In the 2023 placement season, the highest package offered was ?82 lakh per annum (LPA), with undergraduate students receiving an average package of ?21.63 LPA and postgraduate students averaging ?13 LPA. 


The 2024 placement season saw further improvement, with the highest international package reaching ?122 LPA and the highest domestic package at ?110 LPA. A total of 284 job offers were made, including three international positions. college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design and Manufacturing - [IIITDM], Jabalpur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The most important aspect of college is that it is located in heart of India and students from all parts come here making a blend of all Indian cultures , I made a lot of friends and spent a lot of memorable time with them . The college has very friendly environment and ragging free campus , all the senior are very helpful and even professors are often delighted to talk with students about their skills and professional development. The college is one of the 5 central govt funded IIITS of India so it has an affordable fees structure compared to other IIITS and IITS , and an excellent placement activities. The college is located in very remote location at least 10 km away from the Jabalpur city , making it difficult to travel .Literally the delivery cost of Zomato was often high than the price of food itself. The college also suffer with limited campus infrastructure ,inconsistent internet services and less exposure to extracurricular activities compared to other institutes. The weather is extreme in summer days its nearly feel impossible to go to mess from hostel in scorching heat while in winter temperature gets down to 6 degree in morning. | Placement Experience: The coleege placement activities were excellent when we came in 2022 almost 80 to 90 % of students got placed with highest being 110 lpa to avg at 21 lpa . The CSE and ECE branches get more oppurtunities compared to mechanical and smart manufacturing students . The main recruiters were Deloitte, Accenture, Flipkart, Samsung, Amazon, Infosys, Adobe, IBM etc.
However after the recession in 2023 the placed  activities have degraded to some extent.

college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design & Manufacturing - [IIITDM], Chennai
review_title: IIITDM Chennai, a brief review (post-covid improvements).
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Good Infrastructure of boys hostels (I do not know about the girls hostel but it is similar), mess, academics block, laboratory complex and library. Faculty is knowledgeable in their domains and, if studied with heart, they really do teach well. The gym is arguably the best in our campus. It contains almost every machine for individual muscle groups. Recently they have installed AC in our gym. And the sem-wise fees is only 250/-. The campus is good but the ground maintenance should be improved. For eg, the football ground is not covered in grass and contains mostly small stones and pebbles which is why the football players face unnecessary wearing off of their studs. Frequent problems of power cut and LAN issues. Creates big problems during exams. Also the college is in the outskirts of Chennai, so sometimes network problems of SIM also occurs. College is situated in the outskirts, hence sometimes it becomes difficult for shopping and travelling purposes. | Placement Experience: In our 6th sem, companies start to come for internship roles, around march-april. And for full-time roles, companies come in both 7th and 8th sem. Sometimes, these companies also come for internship roles to offer 5th sem students, like Accenture in 2024.
In our college, big companies hardly come for good package (above 15 lpa). Every year, around 5-6 companies come for this package range, mostly for CS students. Then other companies also come like NVIDIA, AMD, Accenture, Infosys, Zoho, Quantrium,  and others as well. You can see the whole data at colleges website.

college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Design & Manufacturing - [IIITDM], Chennai
review_text: Pros/Cons: The junior senior interaction is amazing in this college as most of the seniors are almost always ready to help you out with anything. Almost all the faculty in the college are well established in the field they teach in the college and hence they are able ti clear the doubts we have instantly. On some days of the week the classes start as early as 8 in the morning which is slightly hectic. The library is closed on Sundays which can be irritating but still it is not a huge issue. Due to overcrowding in the gym, they make you pay to buy slots to enter the gym here. | Placement Experience: If you compare the placements here with an IIT or top NIT's, you might find it a little dissapointing but the placements here are very good when you compare it with other top private colleges of our country. The average package stands around 8lpa whereas the highest package went upto 35lpa last year. The students are eligible to apply for placements from the 6th semester and top companis like nvidia and microsoft do pay visits here.My personal plan is to pusue masters in germany after completing my Btech here.You can find further details about the placements in the official site of the institution.

review_text: Pros/Cons: Hostel life is basic but comfortable. Rooms are decent, and the mess offers a mix of Kerala and North Indian food. college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology [IIIT], Kottayam
review_title: IIIT Kottayam at a glance from an insider
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The campus is situated at a scenic location near Valavoor village in Kottayam, Kerala. The view is quite breathtaking, especially during the rainy season.The Summers,on the other hand,can be really harsh. Campus is clean and well maintained. Most of the professors are really helpful. Also seniors take lots of initiatives through various clubs(technical as well as cultural).The curriculum ensures that students are taught and prepared according to the current industrial requirements. Like any other national college, the diversity in the students is quiet amazing. We get to know about people from all the corners of the country. The worst thing about the college is definitely the mess food. I have experienced hostel Life since 5th grade. But never ever I have seen such poor quality food. Vegetarians like me will surely suffer the most. Atleast some fruits should be given... The sports facilities are in absolute shambles. Most of the schools have better facilities than the college. Football and cricket share the same ground(rather just a open,plain and dusty area). Volleyball has a seperate court that gets flooded The outpass system is outrageous considering that this is a college not a boarding school. Now they have introduced online portal for outpass. If you arrive late 3 times than the stipulated time you will not be given outpass thereafter. What is this? | Placement Experience: highest CTC-45 LPA
Average-12.66 LPA
Individual Placements- 126
No.of Offers-169

review_title: A review of Indian institute of information technology senapati Manipur
review_text:
Pros/Cons: There are well equipped Labs(CS, EC,VLSI and Robotics lab) Well qualified teachers. All teachers have P.Hd degrees in their respective field. Mess food is not good. It can be more better. | Course Curriculum Overview: As we all know IIITs are running on new curriculum.
We have lab practicals after completing theory in the classes.the teaching method is good according to me.
The exams are too frequent. 
According to me there should be only one midsem exam instead of 3 assessment .And a end sem exam

Midsem and endsem exams are enough according to me.

college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology - [IIITM], Imphal
review_text:
Pros/Cons: You find few teachers who encourage if you are really interested You can really have a good social life until and unless you are introvert like me No pollution, good environment, peaceful Only senior professor are high knowledgeable and some are not as much as brainy especially the newbie teachers. And that senior professor will some times leave the campus for months to their research works Course curriculum won't match much with your professional development, but this is the case with every college in india It won't provide any good placements or internships like IITS and NIT's | Course Curriculum Overview: Cse ongoing trend, that's why taken that. Curriculum syllabus +few more other topics =placement 
That means the level of your placement depends on your weightage of that few more topics 
For every semester there will be 3 assignment and one final exam. Few days before exam is enough preparation. The course curriculum should change completely for India not just for us and teaching methods are good for senior professor but not juniors

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_title: bits Pilani: pros cons and all other things
review_text: Pros/Cons: the college campus is vast and inculcates a good campus culture. the peer group at bits pilani is great and motivating , the startuo culture in bits is extrmely nurtured they offer good additional courses such as humanities and finance electives that have good course structures and offer knowledge over  arious domains the infrastructure of the classrooms especially the new acadmeic building are upto date and modern as well as fully conditioned the department especially the pharmacy department is extremely rigid and does not fully represent the academic freedom that bits advertisee the coursework is at most times not relevant to the degree (pharmacy) we are made to do courses that have no valuable significance in our degree or our careers the lab conditions are not upto mark we are placed in old labs with expired chemicals and basic amenities such as weighing scales or spatulas are not provided | Placement Experience: the ca,pus placements are above average for the engineering courses in comparision to other private colleges but pharmacy placements are below par tey do not call a lot of core pharmacy companies for placmeents and most people in this degree either opt for non core placements or go for higher studies any core placments have very low packages(4 lpa) and are not much to begin with . i intend to go for higher studies(MBA) either in india or abroad

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_title: BITS Pilani- A relevant review of Pharmacy
review_text:

Extreme heat and extreme cold, hostels rooms are not equipped with ACs. | Placement Experience: The placements in my college are excellent for tech and other domains, however, in terms of my own degree, ie B.Pharmacy, significant direct placement opportunities in core pharmacy are not available. The college is lacking in this respect, even in terms of summer training and opportunities, Pharmacy students do not get much opportunity. Students can also get a ppo from ps2 opportunities. Approx 70-80% people are placed, in terms of pharma, only 30-40% people are placed.

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The startup culture at the campus:  BITS is well known for the startups that it has produced over the years and the startup culture present at the campus plays an important role in building and helping young budding entrepreneurs. Student Faculty interaction : The professors at the campus are quite interactive and they engage with the students and they are easily approachable . Students can easily go and ask professors to work on project under them. Open culture : BITS has quite an open culture , there is no restrictions on hostel entry and exit time , students are quite interactive with each other . There are also many activities continously happening on the campus which engage the students. Girls to boys ratio : the ratio of girls to boys in the pilani campus is approx 1 is to 7 , this number clearly shows that the number of boys and girls on the campus is not balanced Location of Pilani campus: The BITS pilani (pilani campus ) is situated in a small town of rajasthan near the haryana border therefore there is not much to do outside the campus | Placement Experience: For the summer internships the student become eleigible to sit in the recruitment process for the summer internship from 5th semester onwards while for placements the students sit in the interviews from 7th semester onwards.
Many big companies visit the campus like Google , Microsoft , Nvidia , BCG , McKinsey , JP morgan , Goldman Sachs, Tower research , imc trading etc.
Highest package can be close to 1 Cr while average package is 25 lakhs
Around 85% students get placed

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_title: BITS Pilani delivers ; provided the fees fits your budget
review_text: Pros/Cons: Very enthusiastic faculty , impressive publications , some faculty are advisory members in boards liker springer publication The exposure one gets in the college for entrepreneurial ventures as well as finance is immense , if one as an engineer wants to become a financial analyst , the steps are quite easy and set . Zero attendance policy is very beneficial Faculty can take zero attendance more into account . Though there is zero attendance policy there are surprise quizzes which get included in your grade Living amenties are below average at the end of the day Pilani is a village and one does'nt get basic amenities like healthcare and proper food the fees is too much for what they offer , there have been strikes but with no positive outcome | Placement Experience: Students can sit in campus placements starting from their second last semester.Some phenomenal companies that visited placement last year was BCG , Bain n Co . , Jane Street ,Amazon , Flipkart .Also there are many companies /startups which visit for non tech roles like PM / data (Xpedia) and many more .As for the tech and core roles if you have a CG above 8 you will be sure shot placed . Highest packages go upto 2 crores for quant roles since they are highly paid for .Approximately 81 % of the students get placed , I have personally seen students with a CGPA of even 5 get placed a job of 14 Lakhs per annum .My plans after  getting the degree are to work in finance .
