Pros/Cons: Large and well maintained campus spread over 328 acres. Excellent sports facilities ranging from outdoor sports: football, cricket, tennis, basketball, skating as well as indoor sports: badminton, table tennis, chess,etc. Institute's policy of conducting Practice School helps in providing real hands on industry experience for all students which is insightful and gives and edge when appearing for placements. Fees is a bit on the higher side. The institute is private and not government funded which explains the high fees. The course curriculum is a bit outdated and has been the same for the past 10-15 years. Girls to boys ratio is around 1:10 since the institute is merit based and has no reservations. | Placement Experience: For Mechanical branch most of the students were placed(>85%) and average was around 18 LPA. 
Companies like HUL, JSW appear for core mechanical roles.
Highest package offered in the core segment was of 36 LPA in Livguard.
No mass recruiters appear for the placements as students dont feel it's a good ROI to apply in those companies.

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Academic Flexibility: dual-degree program, GPA-based branch changes, option to pursue electives from various disciplines. Faculty and teaching quality: The faculty here is well-qualified with PhDs and research experience. Peer Group: The peer group is smart, diverse and driven. The student-led clubs and departments form the backbone of the campus life. Girls to boys ratio: The gender ratio is skewed, roughly around 1:4 or 1:5. Pilani Location: The remote location is bit of a downside. It's far from major cities which can be inconvenient for travel or industry exposure. However, it creates a tight-knit community and lesser distractions. Mess Food: It's manageable, but nothing to write home about. Most students prefer  food from campus canteens and eateries. | Placement Experience: Placements at BITS Pilani are excellent, especially for branches like CS and EEE. Students become elegible for placements from the 7th semester and most sit for interviews during Practice School (PS-2) semester.
Topo companies like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Atlassian, Qualcomm, Gldman Sachs, BCG, Bain, and DE Shaw visit the campus.

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_text: Pros/Cons: The infrastructure is good, you will get good hostel facilities The faculties are really great with lots of experience and  ready to help at all times The placements are also really good , you can get really good opportunities The curriculum is not that relevant just like all the other colleges The hostel facilities for some hostels are really bad Fees of BITS pilani is too high , just not affordable for some individuals | Course Curriculum Overview: I choose this course because of its relevantness in today's world .CSE is a good branch thats why i opted for it.The exam's frequency is not fixed , sometimes they happen between 1-2 months and sometimes 2 in a month.

college_name: BITS Pilani, Pilani
review_text: Pros/Cons: Alumni culture of BITS is arguably the best . college_name: Birla Institute of Technology and Science - [BITS], South Goa

review_text: Pros/Cons: I liked the modern and beautiful infrastructure, the academic block and the library, of the campus , which provides all the facilities needed for a student. The average package for CS is around 20-26 LPA, for Electrical branches it's around 18-24 LPA and for Mechanical and Chemical it is around 13-16 LPA. These figures would've changed though from the last time I saw. Around 85-90% of the students get placed usually. Top notch companies like Google, Microsoft , DE Shaw, Arcesium Cohesity Rubrick recruit students every year for the tech roles and other prominent companies like Flipkart, Sprinkle, Intuiut and Coin Switch come for managerial roles as well. review_title: Bits Pilani K.K Birla Goa Campus Review
review_text:

review_text: Pros/Cons: The first thing i like here is the infrastructure, it is really great. The classrooms here are excellent and modern. Second is the FFCS(Fully flexible credit system) which allows students to choose their own courses which they have to write in that particular semester. And it feels like you are the boss who decides which class to attend and at what time. Next thing is the Campus life and social interactions. Here you can see students from all corners of India even from other nearby countries. The exposure that you get here is really great and you can enjoy your campus life to your fullest. Things that i disliked at first was as a fresher you are not allowed to go out of the campus(as a hosteller) for first 3months without permission, but later i realised  it is for our safety. The hostel rules might be strict with attendance and discipline and regular checkup will be there if they feel something suspicious about u. the tuition fees might feel a bit high and sometimes a burden to some families. | Placement Experience: B.tech students will be eligible after they complete their credits before the 3rd year and usually that is the period during which they will be eligible for placements. For integrated M.tech it might be after the 4th year in 5th year,they could get a year for internships which is good for students. college_name: Vellore Institute of Technology - [VIT] Chennai, Chennai
review_text: Pros/Cons: Infrastructure - the campus is huge (192 acres). It's well maintained. Every corner there is a canteen. Thee are almost 50plus clubs for both academic and extracurricular purposes. For example I am in AUTOVIT club that does all works related to automation and robotics. I am also part of SEDS,a space club. Variety of events are organised almost everyday People here come from different backgrounds and we get to meet a lot of different kinds of people everyday.for example I met this girl from Bihar and she even made me learn Hindi. This university celebrates everyone with different cultures. Unhygienic maintenance of some canteens. We can see monkeys everywhere using all thoose cups etc. or lizards in water tank.not all places but few need to be maintained properly Almost all Saturdays it's a working day in the name of compensation of classes. We also have exams during sunday if our slot falls on that day. they also sometimes keep exams on afternoon making it very difficult for day scholars. Despite normal bus during morning and evening there is one in the afternoon.only limited bus with certain routed are provided. The crowd during that time while catching the bus is soo much. | Placement Experience: The placements will take place in the main campus I.e Vellore. In 2024 there were 867 companies that visited. Highest CTC 88LPA and average CTC was 9.9LPA. There were a total of 8k offers. The good thing about this college is we will easily get placed in any IT companies. But getting a core job is quite difficult. The regulars are TCS, Cognizant, Nokia, Shell, Intel, PayPal etc. Specfic training's will be provided for all the procedures to be followed during placements. I am planning on doing my masters in aerospace engineering abroad.

review_text: Pros/Cons: the faculty are highly qualified and approachable withgood mix of research expertise and industry knowledge the cmapus infrastructure is excellent with modern classromms ,labs,libraries and hostel facilities the placement support is strong and the peer group is diverse and the curriculum is updated regularly the fees are on the higher side,which may not be affordable for everyone the academic pressure can be high for me its so hectic sometime sit depends that the hostel mess food could be improved in terms of taste and sometime sadministartive processes are really time taking,as we need to go throgh a long process | Placement Experience: at manipla ,mtech students generally become eligible for campus palcements from the 3rd semester onwards ,once they have completed their core technical course and dissertation  work has begun ,a wide range of companies visit teh campus

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_text:
Pros/Cons: College open tipl 10pm u can use the library anytime very welled studying environment around the college Nothing all is good very well maintained community | Course Curriculum Overview: The use of real world case studies in class was beneficial they  helped to show the talend of studiend
As in the exam time usually they make us to feel good in library and labs 
Labs are opened group studies are encouraged

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_title: MIT Manipal is a hot college!!
review_text: Pros/Cons: Not just crowd from all over the country but MIT Manipal has global crowd as well. Clubs of MIT Manipal are a clutch as they help in making connections and opening up. Manipal weather is such that 10 to 3 you will mostly find a weather which is sunny. Because rich brats study here the activities that happens here are beyond management control. | Placement Experience: Banks & companies which are more prone to cyber crimes which including Indian defence too. So, we get the opportunity there as well, and placements obviously are around 10 lakhs and all. It will increase as the industry size grows, and therefore, I believe when we are placed, it will be over 20 lakhs or more.

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_title: Manipal, Student Life You Do Not Want To Miss
review_text: Pros/Cons: Freedom that the college gives you in regards to in-time within campus and within hostel blocks Great infrastructures and buildings with beautiful architectures Help provided in placements by student clubs and organizations Distance between hostels to academic blocks is very long and methods for commuting are limited Course material is not provided until the class has progressed to a certain point making it difficult to get a step ahead Some lab courses are very poorly structured | Course Curriculum Overview: Some subjects in your first year may feel irrelevant and sometimes they are. But most of them provide a better understanding for your own course. In second year you will branch out into your chosen courses and study more indepth topics. For the most part, the course stays on track and remains relevant but some courses are very outdated and could use some improvement as they follow very old standards and teaching methods

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_title: MIT manipal - a complete review(hostel, nightlife, fees, academics)
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus is great, loads of opportunities everywhere, crowd is decent and most of the professors are pretty good. Labs and classes are very well furnished, fully AC. Hostels are very clean and pleasant. Absolutely zero ragging, seniors are very helpful in most cases. Everyone minds their own business. Too hot most of the times, its hard to survive without AC Many places to go to, but gets boring pretty quickly Its a student-run tow, so you wont be having the experience of the real world, everything is easier here | Placement Experience: Placements are decent when taking into account that recession is going on, placement percentage is arond 65 percentage across all branches. For Computer raelated branches its more this year (2025), and median for cs branches is around 12LPA. Students are eligible for placements from 7th sem onwards. Highest package is said to be from microsoft(not confirmed officially) with a package of 52 LPA . Took students from CCE.

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_text: Pros/Cons: Its a beautiful place to study in with so much of nature in and around manipal and the boy girl ratio is a little weird there are so many boys than girls There are a lot of perks like getting discounts in the hospital which is KMC and also everything is free except the medicines on which 10% discount can be availed on being a student The labs, libraries and the teachers are very friendly, and the labs & libraries themselves are really well maintained The weather is unpredictable in the first semester so much of rain, can't wear shoes, all ways have an umbrella and i mean its kinda an advantage and disadvantage cuz one thing the rain makes manipal really beautiful The heat here is so much in the summers, but it makes sense cuz its a costal city I guess you can take it as an advantage and a disadvantage that the campus is big, to get from one place of the campus to another place is an adventure in its own but yes it is quite a journey going to architecture building for a stroll | Placement Experience: From what i have heard the min CGPA required for campus placements is 7 and the Average package was around 10.49 LPA the highest however was 51LPA. The number of recruiters were around 200+ , the companies that visited are Accenture, Wells Fargo, TCS, Deloitte, Amazon, Microsoft, Google and many other companies. The placement rate was around 72% to 73%. My plan after getting my degree is to continue with my job in Australia for around 2 to 3 years and pursue my career in aviation i.e. become a pilot

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_text: Pros/Cons: The Campus and the infrastructure. Hostels are well maintained throughout and facilities are almost perfect The diversity of people in the college is the perfect combination of brain and braun The Student Project culture really helps one if they want to learn a specific skill and also provides great exposure The quality of the crowd, while there are a decent portion of people that are great in academics, still there is a decent portion of the crowd which alienates itself from the academics The strict hostel rules imposed on the students. The Perm Time for 2nd year students is 11:30 pm which kind of restricts the ability of students to do better in their field of expertise. The absurd assignment timings which sometimes clashes with other extra curricular activities in the college | Placement Experience: Students are eligible for placements from the 6th semester and from the 4th semester for internships. Microsoft, JP Morgan, HP, Samsung are some of the companies that visit the college every year. Around 60% people get placed each year from my branch. Highest package is usually 51 LPA and average is around 12 LPA.

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal
review_text: Pros/Cons: Diversity in ppl from states and languages Good girls to boys ratio i dont know the exact ratio though Student Projects are really good and win national competitions High compulsory attendance, its 75 percent High fees and hostel and mess fees are increasing Bad and unpredictable weather, can rain any month | Placement Experience: Campus placements are good as far as i know. The average package is around 9 to 10 lakhs and median is a tad below. Ofcourse its higher for CS branches. Even if u dont get placement from college i think youll be able to get one from outside

college_name: Manipal Institute of Technology - [MIT], Manipal

review_text: Pros/Cons: The infrastructure and the access of utilities to students The curriculum is always up to date and the syllabus is updated regularly. As of now I don't no about the packages and all but it is happening good .I hope that everyone will get the job because all are good students .

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_title: Great Academics, Peaceful Campus — But Not for the Free-Spirited
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Honestly, what stood out to me the most about Amrita was how beautifully it blends academic excellence The campus itself is stunning, especially Coimbatore. It feels peaceful and green, like a place where you’d actually want to spend years studying The strict rules and regulations that made no sense. Favouritism given to local students tamil and especially Malayali. Everyone else was treated very badly. | Course Curriculum Overview: I chose Amrita because the curriculum felt updated and aligned with industry trends. For example, in Computer Science, we had Python from semester 1, AI and ML courses were introduced early
As for exams, we had two internals and one end-of-semester exam per subject. Difficulty was moderate, nothing too surprising if you kept up with classes. The internals actually carried more weight (60–70%).

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_title: Life at Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_text: Pros/Cons: Amrita has a strong faculty base. It's faculty members are from IIT's and NIT's. Students have access to numerous research and project opportunities. Amrita is known for its strict discipline, which may not suit all students. | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for campus placements starting in 6th Semester onwards.Top recruiting companies are Amazon ,HCL, Google,  Microsoft, Oracle, etc. Highest package is Rs 56.95 LPA and average package is Rs 9.2 LPA. Around 75% students get placed. After getting B.Tech degree I am going to pursue my Post Graduation.

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_text: Pros/Cons: every department has experienced faculty, those who have graduated from IITs and hold PHDs Campus was beautiful, very well maintained Fees were low compared to other colleges making it affordable food was better than most colleges but I feel the quality can be improved | Course Curriculum Overview: I took Aerospace Engineering so I took it without having any idea and the course curriculumn made me fall in love with the course over time. Every subject branched out to the different parts of the aerospace industry and really helped us gain theoretical knowledge. We had lab classes as well where we got hands on experience on different equipment.

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_text: Pros/Cons: The best thing about this college is its environment. The trees are everywhere the campus is green There are lots of lab facilities in this college so for the research purposes it is one of the best amongst the private colleges I like the medical facilities also . They provide doctors 24/7 so if you ever fell sick doctors are available The worst part about this college its food. The quality of the food is too bad . One will struggle with the food served here especially The hostel is too old now. They are building new buildings but the ones old are too dark and feel’s like prison. Other facilities like canteen, department store and salon need improvement. Nothing is available in the canteen not even Maggie. | Placement Experience: From the seventh semester only we our placement journey starts. This year our placement was too good. Almost 80% in the civil department got placed which is huge and the average package was around 4.5 lakhs to 5 lakhs. Many big companies like L&T construction, shobha Construction and many others. The highest for the civil engineering is 7.5 lakhs.

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_text: Pros/Cons: The atmosphere around here was awesome the campus is green The canteen and mess food were hygiene compared to other colleges The staffs were very kind and knowledgeable The canteen is overrated and the price is also high The fees are too high that can be satisfied by the low range people Hostel facility was good and the rooms are nice | Placement Experience: With an average of 9 lakh per annum and highest is 56 lakh per annum our college has a placement record .at the placement training our college offers interview preparation, technical skills,soft skills and etc...

college_name: Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore
review_text: Pros/Cons: The environment in the campus is very good for studies The programs and events and the tech fests take place The faculties are well trained and the curriculum is very good The culturals is not upto the level of expectations | Placement Experience: It's your decision to attend placements or not but in my course most of them will consider for abroad studies and college will also provides placement if you maintain good grades but initially the salary will be low

review_text: Pros/Cons: Study wise it was amazing also the placement The people also the environment was not good | Placement Experience: The placement were so bad I mean the company came but they only took sales people rather than coming the finance profile or the operation profile so minimum package was 2 lakh doing mba getting low is quite not good

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_title: A journey of learnig ,friendships and carrer readiness
review_text: Pros/Cons: the campus life was vibrant with active club ,fests and peer groups, social life was good and the girls to boys ratio was around 1:4 ,better than many each other collegess. faculy were qualified ,with most holding Phds .their teaching style was structured and they guided well in projects ,internships , and highjer study pre placement support was strong ,with top firms visiting every yera .traing seasons ,mock test , and resume help made the job process smoother. fee was high and increasd every year , making it through without aid. scholarships was slow at times some labs and hostel areas needed bettteer keepup .wifi was patchy during peak load course was rigid in places , with limited choice of electives and some outdated topics not aligned with latest industry needs | Placement Experience: almost 70-85 % of eligble of students got placeed in reputed firms with CSE and ECE branches achiving the highest ratio .profiles offerred covered software development ,dalta analytics ,consulting ,R&D and core engineering roles .placemnt cell was active ,conducting taring , mocks interviewsd and resume sessions which helped alot in preepartion .

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Teachers, Learning material, Learning method Building Infrastructure, Canteen Food, Library Nothing much, it was a great experience for me. | Course Curriculum Overview: 1. Great research opportunities.
2. There were alot of practical learning, so it prepares you well for the real world.
3. Course curriculum is as per UGC pattern and Teaching Methodology is as per students demands, so we were good on that side.
4. There were mid sem exams and then end sem exams in a semester.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_title: A Valuable Learning Curve with Real-World Exposure
review_text: Pros/Cons: Campus Culture and Community- I enjoyed a well rounded campus life. It's a home away from home that fosters friendships and teamwork across disciplines. Alumni Network- Alumni support referrals ease the placement journey, especially for dilligent students. and talking about the ratio like the ratio is 1:1 Hostel life- Many students appreciate the food quality and the rooms, as the rooms are so spacious and many facilities are given in the hostels. The major thiong that a student would love their is going and sitting with each other after 10. There's a notable absence of a stong student or formal student body, which leaves some students feeliing disconnected from decision- making. 75% attendance requirement, with very few exceptions allowed, limits flexibility. Food options sometime fell repetitive after a while. | Placement Experience: Placement in the college are decent. Mostly students got placed for sales and marketing role. There are very few companies who came for Finance ,HR and Operations and other specializatins. So mostly at the end other specialix=zation students end up with getting a job role in slaes or marketing only.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_title: My imperfectly perfect College life reviewed for everyone who needs it
review_text: Pros/Cons: Sex ratio was good and maintained well, our class was good Exposure was good, other than academics as well Campus was beautiful and teachers were supportive Money i spent on degree due to low package in placement Other than money factor everything was great Could have more stars on thr campus for events like other campuses | Placement Experience: Though when I got admission in this college, I saw the placement rates were very nice, around 95%, but maybe it was our session's fault. I passed out in 2024, and in that particular year, the placements were not as expected and as seen in the previous batch, so that was something very much disappointing because I paid so much money on a degree, only everything was good about the college, but in the end, placement matters during my session. Usually, the companies that hired for example, 10 students only hired two students.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_text: Pros/Cons: the best part of college is the infrastructure such as hostels and grounds that they have. mess food is quite good as compared to other colleges . girls is to boys ratio is also very good . like for our 2027 batch its 1100 boys to 900 girls worst part is that there are many useless subjects in the curriculum such as psychology etc etc also one tging that hostel in time is till 8 30 pm only that the worst part of students life another thing is that there are not enough things in patiala to roam around like you seriously get bored there | Placement Experience: from 6th semester students are leigible for placements. here lots and lots of companies visit. even all these companies dont visit every iit like d.e.shaw,jpmc , morgan stanley etc etc. well d.e.shaw didnt made offer from last 2 years to our campus but its ok there are literally 100 of companies that visit. highest package last year was 54 lakhs as sde in morgan stanley. if you belong to computer branch then placement percentage is almost 90% but for other branches its 50-60%. if i did not get the placement i will be applying for software engineer offcampus.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University], Patiala
review_text:

college_name: Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology - [KIIT], Bhubaneswar
review_text: Pros/Cons: The thing I liked most about my college is the active campus life.KSAC(KIIT student activity centre) is the heart of all the clubs which includes robotics,music etc.The KIITFEST every year is best place to showcase talent and enjoy concert nights. The faculties here are very well educated and approachable.The quality of teaching here is top notch with modern teaching facilities with hands on pratical experience.The mentorship has also been quite impressive. The air condtiotined clasrooms,study rooms and library are well equipped with modern technologies which makes studying, learning easier. The Training and placement cell is also quite supportive and also brings major IT firms to the campus. The amount of distinct students in the campus sometimes makes the things worse,as there is students from across the globe. University should bring more product based and strtup companies rather than mass recruiting companies. The fees of courses are quite high for the courses and experience offered. | Course Curriculum Overview: The curiculum offers a well rounded and industry focused education covering core subjects like C,java,C++,python with modern technologies like Machine Learning,Cloud computing etc.The curiculum focuses on practical application with theoretical knowledge to solve real-world problems.This approach helps students to get ready for industry level works.While the curiculum is good,I suggest there should be more focus on elective subjects such as cybersecurity,block chain etc rather than only the foundational knowledge.Semester exams are held at end of each semester with internal and pratical examination done beforehand.The exams are moderately difficult but theteaching methods and resource provided by faculties make it better to get good grades in exams.

college_name: Symbiosis Institute of Technology - [SIT], Pune
review_text: Pros/Cons: One of the best aspects of this college is that in veery class of every batch, there is a faculty who is appointed as mentor for every 20 students. It's their job to ensure the students attendance, academic performance, and to solve any issues faced. There are continuous assessments for students which makes them brush up their syllabus frequently while the examinations held are under strict vigilance. The professors also assign mini projects which might be individual or team led . There are numerous technical and non-technical clubs via which if a student is attentive and productive might land up on an early internships and many other kinds of lucrative opportunities. There are also events such as debates, hackathons and fests The hostels although safe might sometimes be overwhelming due to some unnecessary rules and regulations. The guards although friendly and supportive, this might not be the same in case of some of the wardens or employees of campus admin office. There are some regulations in campus which might seem less sensible such as not allowing more than 3 people in a room ( not even from the same hostel) . There were some instances where the hygiene of the mess could be called questionable. This was rare but there were such cases. | Placement Experience: Students can apply for campus placements from their 3rd year or the 5th semester. A number of companies usually visit us during placements such as PhonePe, Amazon, IBM, Mahindra, TATA, Dassault, Nvidia and may more. The highest package which I observed was 48LPA 2 years back and the average package is about 9 LPA. The percentage of students getting placements is about 100% IN CSE, 94% in AI&ML, 98% in E&TC. After graduation I plan on getting work experience for a couple years and then go for an MBA degree from a reputed university.

college_name: Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute - [VJTI], Mumbai
review_title: Detailed and Honest Review of VJTI - One of The Oldest Engineering Colleges in India.
review_text:
Pros/Cons: VJTI college is one of the finest colleges in India and Maharashtra. Our college has best faculties, best placements and the events happening here are just awesome. Talking about girls to boys ratio then in my branch there are 24 girls out of 80. Almost every faculty member is a PhD holder. Majority of them are from IIts and foreign Universities. Even some faculties are also research associate at IIT Bombay. Overall campus life is good. Institute crowd is from all over the India. Majority from Maharashtra itself. There are many clubs. 'Rangwardhan' and 'Pratibimb' are the main cultural club. There are clubs for sport, literature, entrepreneurship too. There are two things which I really felt disappointed when I came to this college. One is campus area and other one is infrastructure. Infrastructure is not that bad but not as per what college has its reputation across country. College has 16 acres of campus. which is quite small as compared to other engineering colleges. But having 16 acres of campus in area like South Bombay is impressive. College infrastructure is not that good. But college administration has started renovation almost half of the renovation is done. Some labs, classrooms and hostels are still under renovation. | Placement Experience: 1. From 7th semester students become eligible for campus placements.
2. Companies like Oracle, Texas Instruments and many more visit here. I'm not sure whether Google visited here or not but Google visited here for Internship and one student got selected.
3. Last year's highest package was 57 lpa CTC from cse and talking about average packages then cse-19.71, it-17.38, extc(entc)-11.13, electronics-11.17, electrical-10.43, mechanical-9, civil-6.87, production-7.28, textile-8.28.
4. Percentage of students getting placed are cse-93%, it-95%, extc(entc)-82%, electronics-84%, electrical-95%, mechanical-97%, civil-92%, production-70%, textile-72%.
5. Since I'm in second semester I haven't decided anything. Most probably I will persue higher education.

college_name: Jadavpur University - [JU], Kolkata
review_text: Pros/Cons: I appreciated the importance of education that Jadavpur University always emphasized upon. The faculty and the teaching, the courses have helped me practice my research skills. The department specifically was holistic by invoking extra-curriculars and co-curriculars which included seminars and conferences. These often arrived with the benefits of cultural performances. The university shaped my understanding of the socio-political world and I shall forever remain grateful. It taught me resilience, circumstantial grounds of understanding and helped me become brave enough to remain out of the mainstream and its box. The administration perhaps is the most tedious segment of the entire university. It causes unnecessary delays and can be frustrating. The syllabus for Comparative Literature could use some changes, with respect to including newer aspects of literature, moving away from few of the classically foregrounded courseworks. | Placement Experience: Most of the placements that took place at least for the literature departments ended up with people joining educational institutes and NGOs. Very few joined corporate enterprises like Accenture. But it does provide almost a 5 LPA, if well placed. It is academically rigorous, therefore academics are weighed more than industry.

college_name: Netaji Subhas University of Technology - [NSUT], New Delhi
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Faculty: The faculty is highly knowledgeable and supportive, providing clear explanations and guidance both in and out of the classroom. They have a passion for their subjects, making learning engaging and effective. Gender Ratio: The college has a gender ratio of roughly 10% girls to 90% guys. While imbalanced, this ratio creates a focused and goal-oriented academic environment, which I found conducive to my studies. Placements: The college's placement services are excellent, with a high percentage of students getting placed in reputable companies. The average salary packages are competitive, reflecting the quality of education and career support. Facilities: The college's facilities are outdated and poorly maintained. Labs lack modern equipment, and common areas like the library and sports grounds are in need of significant upgrades. College Area: The campus is quite small and the surrounding area offers very few amenities for students. Its remote location makes commuting difficult and limits access to services and entertainment options. Canteen: The canteen food is disappointing in terms of both quality and variety. The hygiene standards are poor, and the menu is repetitive, making it an unappealing option for daily meals. | Course Curriculum Overview: I liked the scope of the course in the field so I chose the course.
The curriculum prepares us for real worls applications because it's really practical.
The practical exams can be improved.
Exam frequency is okay like once in 2 months and the difficulty is moderate.

college_name: Indian Institute of Science - [IISc], Bangalore
review_title: IISc at a glance: An Institute with a Legacy of Excellence
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Research culture, cooperation and a distinct lack of competition Active student bodies, cultural groups, regional groups Friendly professors, good understanding of mental health on the professor level Bad decisions taken by admin regarding mental health, such as removal of ceiling fans and attempts at coverups. Apparently Caste Based discrimination. Seems to be a lawsuit right now. Difficulty with time management in early semesters. | Course Curriculum Overview: There wasn't really any. It was mostly the friendliness of the profs that pulled me into maths. Do note that in IISc, the first three semesters are common and you pick majors after that.

The maths curriculum prepares you for a future in either industry or academia. Moreover, one can take courses in other departments to help their industry pursuits.

Only way would be to make some courses more interactive, but this hasn't been a problem for me personally.

Most high level courses have two exams. Sometimes not even that. And exams generally aren't difficult for someone who has kept up with the course.

college_name: Indian Institute of Science - [IISc], Bangalore

review_text: Pros/Cons: Great peer group who are just like me, really interested to pursue research Some professors who are very reachable at anytime to ask our doubts Clubs which conduct events to make us learn more about the fields we are interested in It is necessary to study at least three subjects from PCMB for first three years | Placement Experience: Most of the students after completing four years, continue to stay here to get Masters degree after one more year and pursue PhD from world renowned institutions. college_name: Indian Institute of Science - [IISc], Bangalore
review_title: IISc Bengaluru: the premier research institute of India
review_text: Pros/Cons: The infrastructure of the campus: well built labs equipped with the latest technology, libraries with massive up to date collections, big classrooms equipped with projectors, a well built auditorium and sports complex. Campus life: Campus has around 40 running clubs which include various extra curricular activites, regular cultural events are held including music nights, comedy shows and concerts. Faculty: The faculty are really helpful, giving personal doubt solving sessions to whichever student faces any difficulty, apart from giving detailed assignments and quizzes which help in concept builiding Administration: Since the Institute is composed of masters and PhD students comprising 90% of the population, the issues of the Undergraduate community tend to get lesser attention owing to their small population Academic load: Quizzes are held every week, sometimes surprises quizzes are held with no prior information. The assignments given require detailed solutions and contribute to the final grade. Hence the academic load is relatively high here. Construction: Since the undergraduate community is new, several of the builidings are still under construction like the new boys hostel and sports complex | Placement Experience: Campus has a well functioning placement cell which assists students. Students are eligible to sit for placements in the last semesters, given that they have completed the required number of credits. However, a unique point about the institute is that since this is a research oriented institute, most students prefer to go for higher studies. Last year's data shows that only 6 students sat for placements, out of which 5 were placed with an average package of 15 lakhs per annum. The remaining vast majority of students went down two ways: either continuing to do their masters at IISc itself or going for PhD in their field. Out of 100 students, 60 students were accepted into prestigious international universities in the USA, Europe and Australia to conntinue their studies with a PhD and contribute to scientific research. The universities that accepted undergraduates from IISc ranged from University of Michigan to Oxford University to Caltech to University of Los Angeles and many more.

Starting from the 7th semester, students are allowed to participate in campus recruitment, and companies come to the campus for the entire final year. The major recruiting companies are L&T Construction, Shapoorji Pallonji, Brigade Group, Sobha Developers, Atkins, PWD, and Infosys for the non-core sector. Civil students on an average get ?5–6 LPA as salary, and the highest offer is usually in the range of ?12–15 LPA, depending on the company and the position. Almost 70–80% of Civil students are placed, while some of them go for higher studies or government examination.Site engineer, design engineer, planning engineer, survey engineer, and trainee engineer are the core positions that are offered. Also, non-traditional roles in IT and consulting can be taken up by those who are not interested in core engineering profiles.I will first acquire some work experience in the industry after completing my degree and then will pursue a master's degree in structural engineering to gain further specialization.

college_name: MIT World Peace University - [MIT-WPU], Pune
review_title: My Amazing Journey in MIT WPU
review_text: Course Curriculum Overview: This course is very well structured on each and every stream whether it is Finance, marketing, or HR, exam structure is good for this course. In MBA Human Resource the fields that are covered are Marketing management, business communication, managerial economics, Critical thinking, human resource management, legal aspects of business,etc. The second year focuses on specialized courses and industry projects. The student-faculty ratio is very good because of the small strength of the batch. Most of the faculty are IIM Graduates and all of them are Ph.D. holders. The teaching methods used are case-based discussions, classroom activities, learning through videos, group, and individual assignments, writing research papers, and classroom ppts they guide well and clear all topics. The whole course curriculum provides a good amount of theoretical as well as practical knowledge to you. you attend all the lectures carefully and listen to all the things, then you easily pass all the exams.

review_text: Pros/Cons: Discipline. The discipline there is the mainstay behind their academic and professional success of the students. Academic Pressure. college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT], Noida
review_title: A Straightforward Review of JIIT Noida
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Most of the classrooms are clean and the labs also have the basic things needed for practicals. The overall atmosphere is decent for a student. The placement cell is active and they call many companies for internships and jobs. Most teachers have good knowledge and are cooperative. Seniors are very chill and very helpful and really help a lot in making us understand what to do. Some of the facilities like wifi and a few labs stop working at times and it takes time to fix them which gets irritating during exams. Campus life is not very lively and there are not many events or fests compared to other colleges. The curriculum feels very heavy. After every week there's something either lab test, practicals, project submission or our exams. | Placement Experience: The placements in my college are good and most students start preparing from the 3rd year. We become eligible for campus placements from the 7th sem. Many companies come for drives like google, microsoft, jp morgan, atlassian and many more. They offer roles in software, analytics ,web and many core profiles.

The highest package was around 96 lakh this around offered by linkedin and the average package stayed close to 12-13 lakh. Some companies offered 50 lakh packages for software roles and some offered around 30-49 for other corr profiles.
Around 95% of students get placed every year. Some students who do not get placed try off campus or prepare for exams like GATE, UPSC or government jobs or end up doing start-ups.

After completing my degree I plan to get a job as a SDE.

college_name: Dayananda Sagar College of Engineering - [DSCE], Bangalore
review_text:
Pros/Cons: “The college environment is very chill and relaxed, which makes day-to-day campus life comfortable and stress-free.” Academics are chill and manageable, and the exams are also not too stressful, so it’s easy to balance studies and personal “Academics are chill and manageable, and the exams are also not too stressful, so it’s easy to balance studies and personal life.” Faculties even share last minutes notes and questions bank so scoring becomes easy “Placements are below average, and not even 50% of CSE students get placed in good companies. Only a few top students get decent offers while many struggle.” “Most students end up getting placed in mass-recruiter companies or sales and non-tech roles, and very few get strong technical or high-package offers.” Some teachers can be arrogant at times, and their behavior feels disrespectful, which creates an uncomfortable environment for students.” | Placement Experience: Students become eligible for placements from the 6th semester, but the overall placement scenario in DSCE for CSE is below average. Not even 50% of students get placed in good companies. Most students end up getting offers from mass recruiters or in sales and non-tech roles. Only a small percentage of top performers get decent technical packages.

Companies like TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Capgemini, Accenture, Cognizant, and a few mid-tier companies visit the campus, but the number of high-package, core-tech roles is very limited. The average package is on the lower side compared to other top COMEDK colleges.

Because of this, many students plan for higher studies, certification courses, or off-campus drives after completing the degree. The on-campus placement support definitely needs improvement, especially for technical roles.”

college_name: Dayananda Sagar College of Engineering - [DSCE], Bangalore
review_title: DSCE - JOIN AT YOUR OWN RISK
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Well usually the crowd is pretty good, with people coming in from different states and culture. Overall the crowd be it the peers or the seniors are good, approachable and helpful. The location is again not bad, it is in the city so that people can go outside and maybe even enjoy the life in Bangalore. The infrastructure is again decent although it's not a huge campus but has a lot of trees and greenery. Overall decent and even new buildings were getting constructed while I was in the college. Overall decent if not great. At least not bad. I was living in the INDIAN HOSTEL which was  a 4 sharing hostel. It is pathetic with crazy warden. These people are abusive, greedy (being a small word for them). They verbally abuse students and financially put fines for as little things possible. The faculty is not great. Except few decent and helpful teachers rest of them are neither helpful not great at their work. Many good teachers in the faculty even left the college while I was studying in the college with multiple changes within a sem. The administrative staffs are goons. They are incompetent, they forge signature, commit frauds. They harass students, keep on delaying their work, have abusive and non helpful nature. People in govt. offices are better than them. | Course Curriculum Overview: In order to be ready to get a great job, the college and its curriculum is not at all helpful. Most of the students even from other majors are at the end of the day getting job in a coding or computer based company. When it comes to CSE, most of the students were dependent on other platforms to learn DSA, and add other skills of the the domain they wanted to get into. 
SO IN SHORT THE COLLEGE WILL ONLY BE PROVIDING YOU THE DEGREE WHICH WONT LAND YOU THE JOB YOU WOULD WANT. SO BE READY TO TAKE UP MULTIPLE COURSES ONLINE, LEARN FROM DIFFERENT PLATFORMS ONLINE ON YOUR OWN.

college_name: Shiv Nadar University, Chennai
review_title: SNUC - A fresh and modern place to learn
review_text: Pros/Cons: The campus is terrific, it's very very green and the sports facilities are really good. We have an indoor basketball court, indoor badminton court, squash, table tennis, etc. There is a lot of free space to walk and it's not crowded at all. The infrastructure is really good. We have recently been moved into a new block that is fully air conditioned and has centralized AC in all the classrooms and has smart TVs in all classrooms. The library is very useful too. The curriculum is one of the best and advanced. We are being taught all the basics and core subjects that are useful to us from the get go. This helps us focus on our major and gives us more time to excel at it. The college has very few students while that is a good thing it is also a bad thing as there's not a lot of crowd to mingle and interact with. The placement cell is one major drawback. As the college is fairly new and hasn't been fully established yet the placement cell too isn't properly set so there's an uncertainty in that aspect. The college also isn't able to provide it's students with the best internship opportunities yet. There are very few companies that have come and picked out very few students for their internships. | Course Curriculum Overview: The curriculum is one of the best. It is very advanced, updated and modern. They don't waste time on unnecessary subjects and dive right into the core subjects of the speciality. Through this the curriculum prepares us for the real world and helps us focus on what is actually needed to be succesful and get placed. We have exams every month, either CATs or mid sem exams or end sem exams. The difficulty is moderate and not too hard, however it depends mainly on the professor.

some of the top creators for the core branches are Amazon, Infosys , TCS , Wipro, IBM, SAP labs, Cognizant , Tech Mahindra, HCL , JP Morgan Chase , Philips , etc.

the average package for BTech with artificial intelligence and machine learning 4.5 to 6.5 LPA.

college_name: SRMIST Delhi NCR Campus, Ghaziabad
review_text:
Pros/Cons: Girls to Boys ratio is 2:5, There are students from all over Delhi - NCR and other states as well, thus promoting a comprehensive culture within the  college. Faculties have adequate subject knowledge, and use personal notes for teaching. They also regularly provide notes for students to study. The course structure is relevant to the subject. Especially, if you choose a "specialisation" course, the elective subjects add up to be all connected and thus systematic. Regular examinations are conducted : similar to unit tests : (2+3 units), instead of midterms or semester exams. There is certain rigidity regarding college hours for CSE Courses:  9 - 4 regular classes, The course can be a bit too theoretical at times. So students have to continue with their own skill development of revising placement | Course Curriculum Overview: Course curriculum is relevant, especially with AIML currently booming all over the world, the curriculum is highly relevant. 
 Students choosing any course: try out for courses with specialisations: the spec subjects eventually add up and provide more specific domain knowledge.

college_name: SRMIST Delhi NCR Campus, Ghaziabad
review_text:
Pros/Cons: The professors, activities, and hackathons in the college makes it special. Labs are too good in this college. There are more practical works that are done by us with the help of professors. This thing makes this college better than other colleges. There are some placements regarding classes that are too much good for our placements as well as for the skill developments the things that I personally dislike is the timing of this college. Classes starts from 9:20 am and ends at 4:00 pm, which makes it too tough for us to do our personal works. The criteria of 75% attendance is one of the worst thing in this college. The lunch break is just for 40 minutes, which is too bad for students and their health also. | Placement Experience: From 7th semister, student is eligible for the campus placements. If we talk about the internships, student can get the internship offers from 5th semister onwards.
There are many companies that come in this college. there are arouund 1000+ companies that visits college for the placements. These companies includes JP Morgan, Microsoft, Amazon, Boeing, Deloitte, etc. 
The average package is around 7lpa and the highest package is around 45lpa.
The percentage of students getting placements is around 75%. Some students prefers to study further and some leave the placements for the other reasons.
My plan after getting the degree is to be placed in a good company with the good package.
